M_ABSTRACT_SQL_PLAN_OVERVIEW.description: Provides the status of each Plan Stability Manager on every index server in SAP HANA.

STATE.description:
Displays the SQL Plan Stability Manager state:READY: plan stability is enabled.CAPTURE: the abstract SQL plans are being captured.APPLY: the captured abstract SQL plans are being applied for execution plan
generation.IMPORT: the abstract SQL plans are being imported.MIGRATION: the abstract SQL plans are being migrated.UPDATE_LOCATION: the location information of the captured abstract SQL plans is being
updated.

TOTAL_PLAN_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of enabled and disabled loaded plans.

ENABLED_PLAN_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of enabled plans.

DISABLED_PLAN_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of disabled plans.

ALLOCATED_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the allocated memory size, in bytes, being used by plan stability.

LAST_APPLY_START_TIME.description: Displays the last APPLY start time.

LAST_APPLY_STOP_TIME.description: Displays the last APPLY stop time.

LAST_CAPTURE_START_TIME.description: Displays the last CAPTURE start time.

LAST_CAPTURE_STOP_TIME.description: Displays the last CAPTURE stop time.

FILTERS.description: Displays the application filters of the abstract SQL plan.

TOTAL_RELATED_OBJECT_DEFINITION_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of related object definitions.

INVALID_RELATED_OBJECT_DEFINITION_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of invalid related object
definitions.

RELATED_OBJECT_TABLE_SIZE.description:
Displays the allocated memory size of the table, in bytes, being
used for related object definitions.

PLAN_TABLE_SIZE.description:
Displays the allocated memory size of the table, in bytes, being
used for plans.

ABSTRACT_SQL_PLAN_ID.description: Displays the ID of the abstract SQL plan.

PLAN_ID.description: Displays the logical plan ID as a non-negative value.

MIN_EXECUTION_TIME_USING_ABSTRACT_SQL_PL.description: Displays the minimum execution time of the plans generated with abstract SQL plans.

MAX_EXECUTION_TIME_USING_ABSTRACT_SQL_PL.description: Displays the maximum execution time of the plans generated with abstract SQL plans.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME_USING_ABSTRACT_SQL_PL.description: Displays the average execution time of the plans generated with abstract SQL plans.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_COUNT_USING_ABSTRACT_SQL.description:
Displays the total number of executions of the plans generated with abstract SQL
plans.

MIN_EXECUTION_TIME_NOT_USING_ABSTRACT_SQ.description: Displays the minimum execution time of the plans generated by the current optimizer.

MAX_EXECUTION_TIME_NOT_USING_ABSTRACT_SQ.description: Displays the maximum execution time of the plans generated by the current optimizer.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME_NOT_USING_ABSTRACT_SQ.description: Displays the average execution time of the plans generated by the current optimizer.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_COUNT_NOT_USING_ABSTRACT.description:
Displays the total number of executions of the plans generated by the current
optimizer.

LAST_EXECUTION_TIMESTAMP_USING_ABSTRACT_.description: Displays the statistics update time for the plans generated with abstract SQL plans.

LAST_EXECUTION_TIMESTAMP_NOT_USING_ABSTR.description: Displays the statistics update time for the plans generated by the current optimizer.

PROCEDURE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the stored procedure.

STATEMENT_ID.description: Displays the ID of the statement.

STATEMENT_STATUS.description: Displays the status of the statement:EXECUTING: the statement is still runningCOMPLETED: the statement is completedCOMPILING: the statement is compilingABORTED: the statement was aborted

STATEMENT_EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the count of statement execution.

STATEMENT_COMPILE_TIME.description: Displays the elapsed time spent compiling the statement.

STATEMENT_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the elapsed time spent executing the statement.

STATEMENT_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID of the statement.

STATEMENT_TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction ID of the statement.

STATEMENT_MATERIALIZATION_TIME.description: Displays the internal table materialization time.

STATEMENT_MATERIALIZATION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size, in bytes, of the internal table materialization.

STATEMENT_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak amount of memory, in bytes, used for executing each statement inside a
procedure. If you are using a distributed execution, shows the
sum of local peak memory for multiple servers.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for the statement string.

START_MVCC_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the internal MVCC timestamp of the transaction start time.

COMPILED_TIME.description: Displays the compilation timestamp of the statement.

STATEMENT_STATUS.description: Displays the status of the SQL statement.

ALLOCATED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the memory peak used for executing this statement. In case of
distributed execution it is a sum of the local peak memories of multiple
servers.

USED_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Deprecated. This value is the same as ALLOCATED_MEMORY_SIZE.

LAST_EXECUTED_TIME.description:
Displays the recently executed time of the statement. This timestamp is
updated when opening cursors and executing DML/DDL, but not when fetching
cursor results or closing cursors.

LAST_ACTION_TIME.description:
Displays the recently performed time of action against the statement.
This timestamp is updated when opening cursors, executing DML/DDL,
fetching results, and closing cursors.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average statement execution time.

MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum statement execution time.

MIN_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum statement execution time.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the sum of the statement execution time.

AVG_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time of the statement execution including
communication time with the clients.

MAX_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum time of the statement execution, including
communication time with the clients.

MIN_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the minimum time of the statement execution, including
communication time with the clients.

TOTAL_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the sum of the statement execution time, including communication
time with clients.

AVG_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the average memory size, in bytes, used during each
execution.

MAX_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum memory size, in bytes, used during each execution.

MIN_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the minimum memory size, in bytes, used during each execution.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the sum of the memory size, in bytes, used during each
execution.

AVG_LOCKWAIT_TIME.description: Displays the average lock wait time for the statement.

MAX_LOCKWAIT_TIME.description: Displays the maximum lock wait time for the statement.

MIN_LOCKWAIT_TIME.description: Displays the minimum lock wait time for the statement.

TOTAL_LOCKWAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the total lock wait count for the statement.

TOTAL_LOCKWAIT_TIME.description: Displays the accumulated lock wait time for the statement.

AVG_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of statement preparation.

MAX_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of statement preparation.

MIN_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of statement preparation.

TOTAL_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the total time of statement preparation.

TOTAL_PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the total count of statement preparation.

PARENT_STATEMENT_ID.description: Displays the parent prepared statement ID.

STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective statement memory limit.

STATEMENT_THREAD_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective statement thread limit.

TOTAL_STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective total statement memory limit.

TOTAL_STATEMENT_THREAD_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective total statement thread limit.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the effective workload class used for the
execution.

EVENT_TIME.description: Displays the time of the event.

EVENT_REASON.description: Displays the reason for the event.

QUEUE_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the queue wait time, in microseconds.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the unique identifier for an SQL string.

CPU_USAGE_RATIO.description: Displays the measured value of the CPU usage.

MEMORY_RATIO.description:
Displays the measured memory size, in bytes, as a percentage of the global allocation
limit.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the effective workload class

M_ADMISSION_CONTROL_QUEUES.description: Provides detailed information regarding queued session requests by Session-Wise Admission Control.

MESSAGE_ID.description: Displays the request ID of the client packet.

STATEMENT_TYPE.description: Displays the statement type (for example, DDL or DML).

ENQUEUE_TIME.description: Displays the request queue time in microseconds.

TIMEOUT_TIME.description: Displays the estimated time, in microseconds, when the request should be timed-out.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the effective workload class

M_ADMISSION_CONTROL_STATISTICS.description: Provides the overall statistics values of the Session-Wise Admission Control feature.

TOTAL_ADMIT_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated request admission count.

TOTAL_REJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated request rejection count.

TOTAL_ENQUEUE_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated request queued count.

TOTAL_DEQUEUE_COUNT.description:
Displays the accumulated request dequeued count (the executed
request count).

TOTAL_TIMEOUT_COUNT.description:
Displays the accumulated request dequeued count due to timeout
(the rejected request count).

CURRENT_QUEUE_SIZE.description: Displays the current waiting request queued count.

LAST_CPU_USAGE_RATIO.description: Displays the last measured value of the CPU usage.

LAST_CPU_USAGE_MEASURE_TIME.description:
Displays the time at which the last CPU usage ratio was
measured.

LAST_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the last measured memory size in GB.

LAST_MEMORY_RATIO.description:
Displays the last measured memory size, as a percentage of the
global allocation limit
(last_memory_size/memory_allocation_limit).

LAST_MEMORY_MEASURE_TIME.description: Displays the time at which the last memory size was measured.

LAST_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the last wait time of the request in the queue in microseconds.

AVG_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the average wait time of the request in the queue in microseconds.

MAX_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the maximum wait time of the request in the queue in microseconds.

MIN_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the minimum wait time of the request in the queue in microseconds.

SUM_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the total wait time of the request in the queue in microseconds.

AREA_NAME.description: Displays the area of the function.

FUNCTION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the function.

LAST_EXECUTION_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the timestamp of the last execution.

STATE_ID.description: Displays the ID of the state.

AREA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the area.

CREATE_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the time when the state was created.

LAST_ACCESS_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the time stamp of the last access.

FREE_PHYSICAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the free physical memory on the agent host in bytes.

FREE_SWAP_SPACE.description: Displays the free swap memory on the agent host in bytes.

LAST_CONNECT_TIME.description: Displays the last successful connection time.

SYS_TIMESTAMP.description:
Displays the agent host timestamp in the agent host's local
timezone.

USED_PHYSICAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the used physical memory on the agent host in bytes.

USED_SWAP_SPACE.description: Displays the used swap memory on the agent host in bytes.

UTC_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the agent host timestamp in UTC.

M_ANONYMIZATION_VIEWS.description: Provides runtime information about anonymized views.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

VIEW_NAME.description: Displays the name of the anonymized view.

ANONYMIZATION_STATUS.description:
Displays the status of the anonymized view.Possible values for views anonymized using the
DIFFERENTIAL_PRIVACY algorithm are READY, INVALID, or
REFRESHING.Possible values for views anonymized using the K-ANONYMITY and
L-DIVERSITY algorithm are CREATED, REFRESHING, READY, or
INVALID.

REFRESH_RECOMMENDED.description: Displays whether a refresh is recommended: TRUE/FALSE.

LAST_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the finish time of the latest refresh.

LAST_REFRESH_PARAMETERS.description:
Displays the parameters specified during the last REFRESH VIEW command on the view, in
JSON format

M_ASYNCHRONOUS_TABLE_REPLICAS.description: Provides detailed information about asynchronous table replicas. Deprecated.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Schema name of replica (replication target)

SOURCE_TABLE_VOLUME_ID.description: Volume ID of its source table

PART_ID.description:
Partition ID. 0 for non-partitioned tables, 1 through number of
partitions for partitioned tables

REPLICATION_STATUS.description:
Current asynchronous replication status: ENABLED, ENABLING,
DISABLED

M_ATTACHED_STORAGES.description: Provides information about currently attached devices.

PATH.description: Displays the path for the currently attached devices.

KEY.description:
Displays the key for the currently attached devices. This information is
provided by the creator of a specific storage connector.

VALUE.description:
Displays additional information about currently attached devices provided
by the creator of a specific storage connector.

M_BACKUP_CATALOG.description: Provides common data for all backup catalog entries.

ENTRY_ID.description: Dislays the unique ID of the backup catalog entry.

ENTRY_TYPE_NAME.description:
Displays the classification of backup catalog entries. The following
types are supported: complete data backup, data snapshot, log backup, log
missing, differential, and incremental backups.

BACKUP_ID.description:
Displays the unique ID of a data backup or a log backup, respectively.
All backup files of a single data backup share the same BACKUP_ID.

SYS_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time given in the server's local time.

UTC_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time given in UTC.

SYS_END_TIME.description: Displays the stop time given in the server's local time.

UTC_END_TIME.description: Displays the stop time given in UTC.

STATE_NAME.description:
Displays the result of the corresponding action: successful, failed,
running, cancel pending, or canceled.

SYSTEM_ID.description: Displays the system identifier (SID) of the SAP HANA database.

ENCRYPTION_ROOT_KEY_HASH.description:
Displays the key hash used, if any, to locate the encryption root key in
the keystore.

SOURCE_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the database that created the backup.

ES_ENCRYPTION_CHANGE_ENABLED.description:
This column applies to SAP HANA dynamic tiering only. Displays if the
backup can be used to change the volume encryption key during recovery.
Set to TRUE if DISABLE ES ENCRYPTION CHANGE is shown at backup time and
if extended storage is in the topology. Displays NULL if extended storage
does not exist in the topology.

RETAINED.description:
For complete data backups:Indicates whether a backup is flagged as RETAINED.Complete data backups that are flagged asRETAINEDcannot
be deleted by SAP HANA, either individually, by scheduled housekeeping
tasks, or by the SQL statementBACKUP CATALOG
DELETE.

M_BACKUP_CATALOG_FILES.description: Provides location information for all backup catalog entries.

ENTRY_ID.description: Displays the unique ID of a backup catalog entry.

BACKUP_ID.description:
Displays the unique ID of a data backup and log backup respectively. All
backup files of a single data backup share the same backup ID.

SOURCE_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the type of persistence to be backed up: volume or topology.

SERVICE_TYPE_NAME.description:
Displays the type of database service: indexserver, nameserver, or
statisticsserver.

REDO_LOG_POSITION.description:
In the case of a data backup, this value displays the log position that
must be processed next when a log recovery is requested after restoring
the data backup.

FIRST_REDO_LOG_POSITION.description:
In the case of a log backup, this value displays the log position of the
oldest log entry contained in the backup.

LAST_REDO_LOG_POSITION.description:
In the case of a log backup, this value displays the log position of the
newest log entry contained in the backup.

BACKUP_SIZE.description: Displays the size of the backup in bytes.

DESTINATION_PATH.description: Displays that the data or log backup was written to this location.

DESTINATION_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the type of location: file or backint.

EXTERNAL_BACKUP_ID.description: Displays the identifier of the backup received from a backup tool.

LOG_SEGMENT_COUNT.description:
For a log backup, this value displays the number of log segments
contained in the backup.For complete data backups and delta backups, this column always contains
the SQL NULL value.

BACKUP_CATALOG_BACKUP_ID.description:
Displays the unique ID of the backup of the backup catalog that was
triggered after a particular data or log backup.This ID indicates which backup of the backup catalog is the first one
that contains that backup operation.

UTC_LAST_COMMIT_TIME.description:
Displays the most recent commit time in UTC for log backups only.This time shows the most recent point in time, to which this backup can
be used to recover the database.The times of a backup's start and completion do not indicate the point in
time that can be reached in a recovery using that backup. This is because
the most recent database commit is older.

BACKINT_FALLBACK_USED.description:
For third-party backup tools: Indicates whether a log backup was written
as configured, or as a result of an automatic log backup fallback.FALSE: A log backup was written to the file system as configured.TRUE: A log backup was originally configured to be written to a
third-party backup tool, but was written to the file system due to an
automatic log backup fallback.

CONFIGURATION_INCLUDED.description:
FALSE: A data backup contains no user configuration files (.ini
files).TRUE: A data backup contains user configuration files (.ini files).

COMPRESSED_SIZE.description: Specifies the payload size after backup compression.

COMPRESSION_ALGORITHM.description:
Specifies the name of the compression algorithm used for backup
compression.

MAX_RECOVERY_FILE_AGE.description: Displays the maximum recovery file age in seconds.

LOG_REPLAY_STEP_SIZE.description: Displays the log replay step size in bytes.

MAX_RECOVERY_BACKINT_CHANNELS.description:
Displays the maximum number of parallel backint channels per request
during recovery.

BACKINT_EXECUTABLE_LINK.description: Displays the backint executable link name.

BACKINT_EXECUTABLE.description: Displays the backint executable file name.

BACKINT_DATA_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the data backup directory for the backint.

BACKINT_LOG_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the log backup directory for the backint.

BACKINT_CATALOG_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the directory for backint-based catalog backups.

FILE_DATA_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the default directory for file-based data backups.

FILE_LOG_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the current directory for file-based log backups.

FILE_CATALOG_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the directory for file-based catalog backups.

FILE_ROOTKEY_BACKUP_PATH.description: Displays the directory for file-based rootkey backups.

LOG_BACKUP_TIMEOUT.description: Displays the log backup timeout in seconds.

LOG_BACKUP_INTERVAL_MODE.description: Displays the log backup interval mode.

IS_ROOT_KEY_BACKUP_PASSWORD_SET.description:
Displays if the root key backup password is set for the specified
database: TRUE/FALSE.

ROOT_KEY_ENCRYPTION_CONTROL.description:
Displays whether encryption is configured at the system or local
database level: SYSTEM DATABASE/LOCAL DATABASE.

ROOT_KEY_BACKUP_PASSWORD_LAST_CHANGE_TIM.description: Displays when the root key backup password was last changed.

IS_LOG_BACKUP_COMPRESSION_ENABLED.description: TRUE: Backup compression is enabled for log backups.Otherwise: FALSE

LOG_BACKUP_COMPRESSION_ALGORITHM.description: Specifies the compression algorithm to be used for log backups.

LOG_BACKUP_COMPRESSION_LEVEL.description:
Specifies the compression level to be used by the configured algorithm
for log backups.

DATA_BACKUP_COMPRESSION_ALGORITHM.description: Specifies the compression algorithm to be used for data backups.

DATA_BACKUP_COMPRESSION_LEVEL.description:
Specifies the compression level to be used by the configured algorithm
for data backups.

INCLUDE_CONFIGURATION_BACKUP.description:
TRUE: Backup of customer-specific configuration is enabled. Changed .ini
files are included in data backups.Otherwise: FALSE

M_BACKUP_HISTORY_BROKEN.description: Provides information about broken backup history entries.

BACKUP_ID.description: Displays the unique ID of the broken backup history entry.

SYS_TIME.description:
Displays the time at which the broken backup history was detected. The time is provided
in the system's local time.

UTC_TIME.description:
Displays the time at which the broken backup history was detected. The time is given in
UTC time.

SERVICE_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the type of database service.

REDO_LOG_POSITION.description: Displays the redo log position of the broken backup history entry.

REASON.description:
Displays the reason for the broken backup history:Unknown reasonRedo log writing disabled on persistence sessionRedo log flush partially turned OFFRedo log flush turned ONMigration to "level 2 delta"General reason for persistence factoryExtended storage persistence encryption key
changed

M_BACKUP_PROGRESS.description: Provides the progress of the most recent backup.

BACKUP_ID.description:
Displays the unique ID of a data backup. All backup files of a single data backup share
the same BACKUP_ID.

SERVICE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the service.

ENTRY_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the classification of the type of backup.

SYS_START_TIME.description: Displays the local server time that the backup started.

UTC_START_TIME.description: Displays the time that the backup started.

SYS_END_TIME.description: Displays the local server time that the backup was terminated.

UTC_END_TIME.description: Displays the time that the backup was terminated.

STATE_NAME.description: Displays the current state of the backup.

BACKUP_SIZE.description: Displays the total amount of data in bytes.

TRANSFERRED_SIZE.description: Displays the amount of data transferred in bytes.

M_BACKUP_SIZE_ESTIMATIONS.description: Provides the estimated size of the next data backup.

SERVICE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the service.

ENTRY_TYPE_NAME.description:
Displays the classification of the type of backup. The following types are supported:
complete data backup, differential, and incremental backups.

ESTIMATED_SIZE.description: Displays the estimated size of the backup in bytes.

M_BLOCKED_TRANSACTIONS.description: Provides a transaction list waiting for locks.

BLOCKED_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the transaction object ID of the transaction waiting for a
lock.

BLOCKED_UPDATE_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the write transaction ID of the write transaction waiting for
the lock.

BLOCKED_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID associated with the blocked write
transaction.

LOCK_OWNER_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the transaction object ID of the transaction holding the
lock.

LOCK_OWNER_UPDATE_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the write transaction ID of the write transaction holding the
lock.

LOCK_OWNER_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID associated with the write transaction holding
the lock.

WAITING_RECORD_ID.description: Displays the ID of the record on which the lock is currently placed.

WAITING_SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the schema on which the lock is currently
placed.

WAITING_OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the object on which the lock is currently
placed.

WAITING_PART_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the locked partition, or 0 for a non-partition
lock.

LOCK_TYPE.description:
Displays the lock type: RECORD, TABLE, or METADATA. The active lock and
the requested lock always have the same lock type. This value applies to
the lock, not the request.

LOCK_MODE.description:
Displays the lock mode: SHARED, EXCLUSIVE, or INTENTIONAL EXCLUSIVE.
LOCK_MODE applies to the active lock.

M_BUFFER_CACHE_POOL_STATISTICS.description: Provides statistics for each buffer pool in a cache.

IO_READ_SIZE.description: Displays the disk I/O reads over time.

MISS_COUNT.description: Displays the cache miss count of the cache group.

VOLUME_ID.description: Displays the volume identifier for the index server.

CACHE_NAME.description: Displays the cache name: C/S, R/S.

BUFFER_SIZE.description: Displays the size of the buffer in the pool in bytes.

REPLACEMENT_POLICY.description: Displays the type of replacement policy used by the cache.

GROWTH_PERCENT.description: Displays the rate, as a percentage, at which the buffer pool can grow:TOTAL_BUFFER_COUNT = TOTAL_BUFFER_COUNT + GROWTH PERCENT / 100 * MAX_SIZE

TOTAL_BUFFER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of buffers allocated to the pool.

FREE_BUFFER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of free buffers for the pool.

LRU_LIST_BUFFER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of buffers in the LRU chain for the pool.

HOT_BUFFER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of buffers in the hot buffer list for the pool.

BUFFER_REUSE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of buffers released from the LRU list for the pool so that a
requested page can be cached.

OUT_OF_BUFFER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of times that an out-of-buffer situation occurred while requesting
buffers from the pool.

M_BUFFER_CACHE_STATISTICS.description: Provides a cache level overview of the configuration, cache status, and memory usage.

IO_READ_SIZE.description: Displays the disk I/O reads over time.

VOLUME_ID.description: Displays the volume identifier for the index server.

CACHE_NAME.description: Displays the cache name: C/S or R/S.

STATE.description:
Displays the cache state: ENABLED, DISABLING, DISABLED, or
SHRINKING.

REPLACEMENT_POLICY.description: Displays the type of replacement policy used by the cache.

MAX_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum buffer cache memory capacity in bytes.

MISS_COUNT.description: Displays the cache miss count of the cache group.

ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the allocated memory for the buffer cache in bytes.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used memory in buffer cache in bytes.

BUFFER_REUSE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of times that a buffer is released for reuse
by the cache.

HIT_RATIO.description:
Displays the ratio of pages found in the buffer cache to pages
requested from the buffer cache.

CACHE_ID.description: Displays the unique identifier for the cache.

TOTAL_SIZE.description:
Displays the maximum available memory budget in bytes available for the
cache instance.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the memory in bytes used by the cache instance.

ENTRY_COUNT.description: Displays the number of entries in the cache instance.

INSERT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of insertions into the cache instance.

INVALIDATE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of invalidations in the cache instance.

HIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of cache hits for the cache instance.

MISS_COUNT.description: Displays the number of cache misses for the cache instance.

LAST_ACCESS_TIME.description: Displays the time of the last access of the cache instance.

IS_PERSISTENT.description:
Indicates if the calculation scenario is persistent or transient: TRUE or
FALSE.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time.

MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the memory size of the loaded calculation scenario model in
bytes.

COMPONENT.description: Displays the component that created the calculation scenario.

SCENARIO_VERSION.description: Displays the scenario version of the calculation scenario.

JSON.description: Displays the JSON string representing the calculation scenario.

M_CE_CALCVIEW_DEPENDENCIES.description: Provides all views that are referencing a CalculationScenario.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the column view.

CALCNODE_NAME.description: Displays the referenced calculation node name.

M_CE_DEBUG_INFOS.description: Provides debug information after the execution of a calculation scenario.

DEBUG_TYPE_MASK.description: Displays the bit mask indicating the type of debug information.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the table name (temporary intermediate result table).

TABLE_SIZE.description:
Displays the table size in bytes (temporary intermediate result
table).

M_CE_DEBUG_JSONS.description: Provides all available JSONS (original, instantiated, or optimized) of a scenario for a concrete query.

TYPE.description:
Displays the JSON type: original, instantiated, optimized, or
extrace.

MODEL_JSON.description: Displays the JSON string representing the calculation scenario.

M_CE_PLE_CALCSCENARIOS.description: Provides all available calculation scenarios created by the PlanningEngine.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of calculation scenario.

M_CLIENT_VERSIONS.description: Provides versions of all supported client applications.

CLIENT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of client, for example, ABAP_FDA or BI Modeler.

CLIENT_RELEASE_DESC.description: Displays the human readable release description.

MIN_VERSION.description: Displays the lowest supported protocol version.

MAX_VERSION.description: Displays the highest supported protocol version.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

COLLECTION_ID.description: Displays the ID of the collection.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time of the table collection.

ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the allocated size of the collection in bytes.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used size of the collection in bytes.

LOAD_STATUS.description: Displays whether the table collection is LOADED or UNLOADED.

M_COLLECTION_TABLE_VIRTUAL_FILES.description: Provides information about the virtual files for JSON collections.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

COLLECTION_ID.description: Displays the ID of the collection.

INTERNAL_OBJECT_ID.description: Displays the internal identifier for the table.

PHYSICAL_SIZE.description: Displays the storage size used for the file in bytes.

PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of storage pages used for the file.

NUM_COMPACTION_COLLISIONS.description:
Displays the count of memory compaction collisions, which includes other
threads currently in compaction and unforced compactions.

NUM_COMPACTIONS.description: Displays the number of compaction requests.

LAST_SIZE_COMPACTION_REQUESTS.description: Displays the size in bytes of the last compaction request.

MAX_SIZE_COMPACTION_REQUESTS.description: Displays the maximum sized compaction request in bytes.

MIN_SIZE_COMPACTION_REQUESTS.description: Displays the minimum sized compaction request in bytes.

SUM_SIZE_COMPACTION_REQUESTS.description: Displays the total size of compaction requests in bytes.

AVG_SIZE_COMPACTION_REQUESTS.description: Displays the average size of compaction requests in bytes.

LAST_SIZE_FREEABLE.description: Displays the size in bytes compacted by the last at compaction call.

MAX_SIZE_FREEABLE.description: Displays the size in bytes compacted by the maximum compaction call.

MIN_SIZE_FREEABLE.description: Displays the size in bytes compacted by the minimum compaction call.

SUM_SIZE_FREEABLE.description: Displays the size in bytes compacted by all compaction calls (total).

AVG_SIZE_FREEABLE.description: Displays the size in bytes compacted by the average compaction call.

LAST_SIZE_FREED_BY_GARBAGE_COLLECTION.description:
Displays the size in bytes compacted by the last memory garbage
collection (defragmentation).

MAX_SIZE_FREED_BY_GARBAGE_COLLECTION.description:
Displays the size in bytes compacted by the largest memory garbage
collection (defragmentation).

MIN_SIZE_FREED_BY_GARBAGE_COLLECTION.description:
Displays the size in bytes compacted by the smallest memory garbage
collection (defragmentation).

SUM_SIZE_FREED_BY_GARBAGE_COLLECTION.description:
Displays the size in bytes compacted by all memory garbage collections
(defragmentation) (total).

AVG_SIZE_FREED_BY_GARBAGE_COLLECTION.description:
Displays the size in bytes compacted by the average memory garbage
collection (defragmentation).

LAST_COMPACTION_RESULT.description:
Displays the last compaction result in bytes. This is the difference of
allocated bytes before and after compaction and may be influenced by
other factors than compaction.

MAX_COMPACTION_RESULT.description:
Displays the largest compaction result in bytes. This is the difference
of allocated bytes before and after compaction and may be influenced by
other factors than compaction.

MIN_COMPACTION_RESULT.description:
Displays the smallest compaction result in bytes. This is the difference
of allocated bytes before and after compaction and may be influenced by
other factors than compaction.

SUM_COMPACTION_RESULT.description:
Displays the total of all compaction results in bytes. This is the
difference of allocated bytes before and after compaction and may be
influenced by other factors than compaction.

AVG_COMPACTION_RESULT.description:
Displays the average compaction result in bytes. This is the difference
of allocated bytes before and after compaction and may be influenced by
other factors than compaction.

STATISTICS_ID.description: Displays the statistics object unique ID.

WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the count of wait calls.

BLOCKING_COUNT.description: Displays the count of blocking wait calls.

LAST_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the latest time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

MAX_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

MIN_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

SUM_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the total time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

AVG_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the average time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

CREATE_COUNT.description:
Displays the count of semaphore creations (for shared statistics
only).

DESTROY_COUNT.description:
Displays the count of semaphore destructions (for shared statistics
only).

SECTION.description: Displays the section of the parameter.

HAS_KEY_INDEX.description: Displays whether or not the parameter is an indexed parameter: TRUE/FALSE.

FILE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the file where the parameter value is defined.

LAYER_NAME.description: Displays that layer that the parameter is defined on.

HAS_PROPERTIES.description: Displays whether or not the parameter has properties: TRUE or FALSE.

RAW_VALUE.description: Displays the value of the configuration parameter, as stored in the ini file.

VIOLATED_RESTRICTIONS.description:
Displays the restrictions violated by the set value: CUSTOM, VALUE_RESTRICTION, or
LAYER_RESTRICTION.

RESTART_REQUIRED.description:
Displays if the parameter value has been changed and requires a restart to become
effective: TRUE/FALSE.

M_CONNECTIONS.description: Provides detailed information on connections between a client and a database. Information includes: connection status, client information, connection type, and resource utilization.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction object ID.

START_TIME.description: Displays the connected time.

IDLE_TIME.description:
Displays the time that the connection is unused and idle in
milliseconds.

CONNECTION_STATUS.description:
Displays the connection status:RUNNINGA statement is executing.IDLENo statements are currently executing on this connection.QUEUEINGThe connection is currently queued. The status changes to
RUNNING when it is dequeued (this depends on the system's
resource consumption).EMPTYA historic connection that is removed after 1 hour. The removal
time can be configured inindexserver.ini [session]
connection_history_lifetime = 60 # minutes.

CLIENT_HOST.description: Displays the host name of client machine.

CLIENT_IP.description: Displays the IP of client machine.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

CONNECTION_TYPE.description:
Displays the connection type. Connection types include: Remote, Local,
History (remote), and History (local).

OWN.description:
Displays the user's own connection. Returns TRUE if it is your own
connection and FALSE if it is not.

MEMORY_SIZE_PER_CONNECTION.description: Displays the allocated memory size per connection in bytes.

AUTO_COMMIT.description:
Displays the commit mode of the current transaction. Returns TRUE if the
current connection is in auto-commit mode and FALSE if it is not.

LAST_ACTION.description:
Displays the last action completed by the current connection. Actions
include: ExecuteGroup, CommitTrans, AbortTrans, PrepareStatement,
CloseStatement, ExecutePrepared, ExecuteStatement, FetchCursor,
CloseCursor, LobGetPiece, LogPutPiece, LobFind, Authenticate, Connect,
Disconnect, ExecQidItab, CursorFetchItab, InsertIncompleteItab,
AbapStream, TxStartXA, and TxJoinXA.

FETCHED_RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the sum of the record count fetched by select statements.

AFFECTED_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the sum of the record count affected by DML/DDL statements, for
example:INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/REPLACE/CREATE TABLE ... LIKE ... WITH DATA/CREATE TABLE ... AS (SELECT ..)/IMPORT/EXPORT

SENT_MESSAGE_SIZE.description:
Displays the total size of messages sent by the current connection in
bytes.

SENT_MESSAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the total message count sent by the current connection.

RECEIVED_MESSAGE_SIZE.description:
Displays the total size of messages received by the current connection in
bytes.

RECEIVED_MESSAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the total message count received by the current connection.

CREATOR_THREAD_ID.description: Displays the thread ID that created the current connection.

CREATED_BY.description:
Displays the engine component that created the connections. These
include: Session, Planning, Repository, CalcEngine, Authentication, Table
Exporter, Loader, LLVM, JSVM, IMS Search API, OLAP Engine, Mergedog, Ping
Status, Name Server, Queue Server, SQL Stored Procedure, Authorization,
TrexViaDbsl from ABAP, HybridTable Reorganizer, and Session external.

IS_ENCRYPTED.description:
Displays whether or not the connection is encrypted with secure
communication enabled (SSL enabled): TRUE/FALSE.

SSL_VERSION.description:
Displays the SSL/TLS protocol version.The result is empty if the connection does not use SSL (IS_ENCRYTED) or
if the SSL_VERSION contains the used SSL protocol version.

SSL_CIPHER.description: Displays the SSL cipher used for the connection.The result is empty if SSL_CIPHER contains the used ciphersuite.

HAS_SSL_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE.description:
Displays whether mutual authentication is enabled: TRUE/FALSE. TRUE
indicates that the secure communication is enabled (SSL enabled) and the
certificate of the client is validated. If not, FALSE is returned.

AUTHENTICATION_METHOD.description:
Displays the name of the authentication method used to authenticate the
connection.

END_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the connection is closed for history
connections.

CLIENT_DISTRIBUTION_MODE.description:
Displays the client distribution mode of the current connection (for
example, [distribution] client_distribution_mode).

LOGICAL_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the logical connection ID in statement routing.

SOURCE_SITE_LOGICAL_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the logical connection ID of the origin site.

CURRENT_THREAD_ID.description: Displays the current executing thread ID.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the effective workload class.

CURRENT_COLLATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the current collation.

CLOSE_REASON.description: Displays the reason for the connection close.

M_CONNECTION_STATISTICS.description: Provides detailed statistics on each connection between an application and database.

SELECT_EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of select statement executions.

SELECT_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total execution time of select statement executions.

SELECT_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average execution time of select statement executions.

SELECT_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum execution time of select statement executions.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of select for update executions.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total execution time of select for update executions.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average execution time of select for update executions.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum execution time of select for update execution.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock waits during select for update.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the total lock wait time during select for update.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_AVG_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the average lock wait time during select for update.

SELECT_FOR_UPDATE_MAX_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the maximum lock wait time during select for update.

UPDATE_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of update statement and insert statement
executions.

UPDATE_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description:
Displays the total execution time of update statement executions in
microseconds.

UPDATE_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description:
Displays the average execution time of update statement executions in
microseconds.

UPDATE_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum execution time of update statement executions in
microseconds.

UPDATE_LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock waits during update statement executions.

UPDATE_TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the total lock wait execution time during update statement
executions in microseconds.

UPDATE_AVG_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the average lock wait time during update statement executions in
microseconds.

UPDATE_MAX_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum lock wait time during update statement executions in
microseconds.

READ_ONLY_TRANSACTION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of read only transactions.

READ_ONLY_TRANSACTION_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TI.description:
Displays the total execution time of read only transactions in
microseconds.

READ_ONLY_TRANSACTION_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description:
Displays the average execution time of read only transactions in
microseconds.

READ_ONLY_TRANSACTION_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum execution time of read only transactions.

UPDATE_TRANSACTION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of update transactions.

UPDATE_TRANSACTION_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total execution time of update transactions.

UPDATE_TRANSACTION_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average execution time of update transactions.

UPDATE_TRANSACTION_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum execution time of update transactions.

ROLLBACK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of rolled backed transactions.

ROLLBACK_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total execution time of rollbacks.

ROLLBACK_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average execution time of rollbacks.

ROLLBACK_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum execution time of rollbacks.

OTHERS_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of other statement executions including data
definition statements and data control statements.

OTHERS_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total execution time of other statements.

OTHERS_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average execution time of other statements.

OTHERS_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum execution time of other statements.

OTHERS_LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the total lock wait count of other statements.

OTHERS_TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the total lock wait time of other statements.

OTHERS_AVG_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the average lock wait time of other statements.

OTHERS_MAX_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the maximum lock wait time of other statements.

LAST_EXECUTED_TIME.description: Displays the last execution timestamp with this connection.

AVG_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the average memory size used during each execution in bytes.

MAX_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum memory size used during each execution in bytes.

MIN_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the minimum memory size used during each execution in bytes.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the sum of the memory size used during each execution in
bytes.

AVG_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the statement preparation.

MAX_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the statement preparation.

MIN_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of the statement preparation.

TOTAL_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the total time of the statement preparation.

TOTAL_PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the total count of statement preparation.

EXECUTION_COUNT_BY_ROUTING.description:
Displays the execution count by the client routed connection in statement
routing.

COMMIT_MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the commit execution.

COMMIT_AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the commit execution.

COMMIT_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total time of the commit duration.

END_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the connection is closed for history
connections.

CHECK_EXECUTION_ID.description: Displays the unique identifier for a run.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID of the last check execution.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the ID of the user performing the consistency check.

CHECK_PROCEDURE_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the check procedure, for example
CHECK_TABLE_CONSISTENCY.

CHECK_ACTION.description:
Displays the single check action or a comma-separated list of
check actions.

SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the schema name. NULL is used if all schemas were
checked.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the object.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the object.

CHECK_EXECUTION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of executions related to the aggregated
entry.

FIRST_START_TIME.description:
Displays the first start time of an aggregated entry. For an
aggregated entry without any errors, this value defines the
interval in which all checks were error-free.

LAST_START_TIME.description:
Displays the last invocation start time of an aggregated entry.
For an aggregated entry without any errors, this value defines
the interval in which all checks were error-free.

LAST_DURATION.description:
Displays the total duration of the last check in
milliseconds.

MIN_DURATION.description:
Displays the minimum duration of the check runs in
milliseconds.

MAX_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum duration of the check runs in
milliseconds.

AVG_DURATION.description: Displays the average duration of the check runs milliseconds.

LAST_SCHEDULED_TABLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of tables scheduled for checking.

MIN_SCHEDULED_TABLE_COUNT.description:
Displays the minimum number of scheduled table counts for the
checks runs.

MAX_SCHEDULED_TABLE_COUNT.description:
Displays the maximum number of scheduled table counts for the
checks runs.

AVG_SCHEDULED_TABLE_COUNT.description:
Displays the average number of scheduled table counts for the
checks runs.

EXECUTED_TABLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of tables that have been checked.

ERROR_TABLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of tables with errors.

ERROR_CODE.description: Displays the error code returned by the check.

ERROR_MESSAGE.description: Displays the error message returned by the check.

M_CONSISTENCY_CHECK_HISTORY_ERRORS.description: Lists the errors that were found within a specified check run.

CHECK_EXECUTION_ID.description:
Displays the execution ID of the consistency check run that
produced the error.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the object.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the object.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Contains 0 for an unpartitioned
table.

SEVERITY.description: Displays the error severity: LOW, INFO, HIGH, or ERROR.

CHECK_ACTION.description:
Displays the check action that produced the error. Contains NULL
for errors not related to a specific check action.

AFFECTED_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows affected by the error (optional).This column does not always contain a value, even in the case of
an error. For some errors, this column may contain a NULL
value.

DETAILS.description:
Displays the detailed information related to the error
(optional).This column does not always contain a value, even in the case of
an error. For some errors, this column may contain a NULL
value.

CNT_CREATE_ROLLBACK.description: Displays the number of rolled back creates.

CNT_REMOVE_FAIL.description: Displays the number of failed removes.

CNT_REMOVE_ROLLBACK.description: Displays the number of rolled back removes.

CNT_GET.description: Displays the number of retrieved containers.

CNT_GET_FAIL.description: Displays the number of failed gets.

CNT_BEGIN.description: Displays the number of used iterators.

CNT_ITERATED.description: Displays the number of iterated containers.

CNT_CACHEHIT.description: Displays the number of cache hits.

CNT_CACHEMISS.description: Displays the number of cache misses.

CNT_CONTAINERS.description: Displays the number of existing containers.

STATISTICS_ID.description: Displays the statistics object unique ID.

INCLUSIVE_SIZE_IN_USE.description:
Displays the current size of this allocator, including suballocators in
bytes.

INCLUSIVE_COUNT_IN_USE.description:
Displays the number of blocks currently in use, including
suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description:
Displays the total allocated size in this allocator and suballocators in
bytes.

INCLUSIVE_DEALLOCATED_SIZE.description:
Displays the total deallocated size in this allocator and suballocators
in bytes.

INCLUSIVE_ALLOCATED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of allocations, including suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_DEALLOCATED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of deallocations, including suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_MAX_SINGLE_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description:
Displays the largest block size, in bytes, ever allocated in this
allocator and suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_PEAK_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description:
Displays the largest size of this allocator and suballocators (estimate)
in bytes.

INCLUSIVE_LIMIT.description:
Displays the maximum allowed memory size of the specified allocator and
suballocators in bytes. The limit is not a hard limit and may therefore
be violated slightly. It is not possible to set limits for each allocator
individually.

INCLUSIVE_IN_USE_INTEGRAL.description:
Displays the average allocated memory by this allocator and its
suballocators, multiplied by the time since the start of measurement
(sample based rough estimate). Deactivated by default, this value should
only be activated upon request by SAP support. The UOM is 1 byte times 1
second.

EXCLUSIVE_SIZE_IN_USE.description: Displays the current size of this allocator in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_COUNT_IN_USE.description: Displays the number of blocks currently in use.

EXCLUSIVE_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the total allocated size in this allocator in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_DEALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the total deallocated size in this allocator in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_MAX_SINGLE_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description:
Displays the largest block size, in bytes, ever allocated in this
allocator.

EXCLUSIVE_PEAK_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description: Displays the largest size of this allocator (estimate) in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_ALLOC_ERRORS.description: Displays the number of allocation errors.

EXCLUSIVE_IN_USE_INTEGRAL.description:
Displays the average allocated memory by this allocator in byptes,
multiplied by the time since the start of measurement (sample based rough
estimate), Usually deactiveate, this value should only be activated at
the requested by SAP support. The UOM is 1 byte times 1 second.

COMPONENT.description: Displays the name of the SAP HANA component of this allocator.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the effective workload class.

TYPE.description: Displays the type of converter.

MAX_LEVEL.description: Displays the maximum level. For example, the root page level.

MAX_PAGENUMBER.description: Displays the maximum page number in HEXID.

ALLOCATED_PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of currently allocated pages.

ALLOCATED_PAGE_SIZE.description: Displays the total size, in bytes, of the currently allocated pages.

MAX_ALLOCATED_PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the maximum number of allocated pages.

MAX_ALLOCATED_PAGE_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum size, in bytes, of the allocated pages.

ALLOCATE_PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of page allocations.

ALLOCATE_OR_GET_STATIC_PAGE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of page allocations or retrievals during the static
phase.

DEALLOCATE_PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of page deallocations.

ASSIGN_PHYSICAL_PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of physical page assignments.

UNASSIGN_PHYSICAL_PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of physical page unassignments.

UNASSIGN_PHYSICAL_PAGE_COUNT_DURING_DROP.description:
Displays the number of physical page unassignments during a drop
snapshot.

CREATE_SNAPSHOT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of created snapshots.

DROP_SNAPSHOT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of dropped snapshots.

WRITE_CONVERTERPAGE_LEVEL0_COUNT.description: Displays the number of level 0 converter pages written to the disk.

WRITE_CONVERTERPAGE_LEVEL1_COUNT.description: Displays the number of level 1 converter pages written to the disk.

WRITE_CONVERTERPAGE_LEVEL2_COUNT.description: Displays the number of level 2 converter pages written to the disk.

WRITE_CONVERTERPAGE_LEVEL3_COUNT.description: Displays the number of level 3 converter pages written to the disk.

WRITE_CONVERTERPAGE_LEVEL4_COUNT.description: Displays the number of level 4 converter pages written to the disk.

M_CS_ALL_COLUMNS.description: Provides runtime information for all columns in column tables, including internal column tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Returns the table partition ID:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.For replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original table, and
subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.The part ID -1 indicates that the table schema is being
modified.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description:
Displays the sum of the MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN and MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA
columns in bytes.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN.description:
Displays the current memory consumption in main in bytes. Returns 0 if
the memory is not loaded in main.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA.description:
Displays the current memory consumption in delta in bytes. Returns 0 if
the memory is not loaded in delta.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_PAGE_LOADABLE_MAIN.description: Displays the total paged memory size of the column in bytes.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description:
For columns stored in persistent memory, displays the total memory size
in bytes.

UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE.description: Displays the estimated, uncompressed column size in bytes.

COMPRESSION_RATIO_IN_PERCENTAGE.description:
Displays the compression ratio percentage. This value is determined by
dividing the MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL by the UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE and
multiplying the result by 100.

COUNT.description: Displays the record count. Returns 0 if the count is not loaded.

DISTINCT_COUNT.description:
Displays the distinct count of values. Returns 0 if the count is not
loaded.

DISTINCT_COUNT_IN_MAIN.description: Displays the number of distinct values in MAIN. -1 if not loaded.

DISTINCT_COUNT_IN_DELTA.description: Displays the number of distinct values in DELTA. -1 if not loaded.

COMPRESSION_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of column compression. This value can be: SPARSE,
PREFIXED, CLUSTERED, INDIRECT, RLE, or DEFAULT (if the column is only
dictionary coded). The columns in the M_CS_COLUMNS system view show the
runtime value, which can be changed during the runtime.

INDEX_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of inverted index:FULLDisplays that a FULL inverted index is defined on the column. A
FULL index is a data structure that assigns a position to each
value in the column. FULL indexes are the conventional form of
index.BLOCKDisplays that a block index is defined on the column.A BLOCK index is a data structure where each value in the column
is assigned to one of a list of fixed-size blocks for the
column. BLOCK indexes are typically much smaller than FULL
indexes. Reverse look-ups on BLOCK indexes are effectively a
combination of reading the index (fast) and scanning the read
blocks (possibly slow, depending on the compression of the
column). BLOCK indexes can be used with compressed columns.MINMAXDisplays that a min-max index is defined for the column.NONEDisplays that no index is defined for the column.

INDEX_LOADED.description:
Displays the load status of the inverted index: NOT APPLICABLE, UNLOADED,
or LOADED.

IMPLEMENTATION_FLAGS.description: Displays the column internal implementation specification summary.

LAST_ACCESS_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the column was read or there was an INSERT
to, UPDATE of, or DELETE from the column. This value is undefined for
unloaded columns.

LOADED.description:
Displays whether the flag to indicate that the column is loaded into
memory (including DRAM or persistent memory): TRUE/FALSE.

LOADED_FROM_PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays whether the column was loaded from persistent memory:
TRUE/FALSE.

STORED_IN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays whether there is an associated persistent memory block:
TRUE/FALSE.

LOAD_UNIT.description: Displays the load unit for the columns: PAGE, COLUMN, and UNKNOWN.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

MAIN_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DATA.description: Displays the current memory consumption in the data in bytes.

MAIN_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DICT.description: Displays the current memory consumption in the dictionary in bytes.

MAIN_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_INDEX.description: Displays the current memory consumption in the index in bytes.

MAIN_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MISC.description: Displays the miscellaneous current memory consumption in bytes.

DELTA_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DATA.description: Displays the current memory consumption in the data in bytes.

DELTA_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DICT.description: Displays the current memory consumption in the dictionary in bytes.

DELTA_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_INDEX.description: Displays the current memory consumption in the index in bytes.

DELTA_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MISC.description: Displays the miscellaneous current memory consumption in bytes.

MAIN_PAGE_LOADABLE_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DATA.description:
Displays the total paged memory size of the column index vector in
bytes.

MAIN_PAGE_LOADABLE_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DICT.description: Displays the total paged memory of the column dictionary in bytes.

MAIN_PAGE_LOADABLE_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_INDEX.description:
Displays the total paged memory of the column's inverted index in
bytes.

MAIN_PAGE_LOADABLE_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MISC.description:
Displays the total paged memory size of other column structures in
bytes.

MAIN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DATA.description:
For columns stored in persistent memory, displays the current memory
consumption in the data in bytes.

MAIN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DICT.description:
For columns stored in persistent memory, displays the current memory
consumption in the dictionary in bytes.

MAIN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_INDEX.description:
For columns stored in persistent memory, displays the current memory
consumption in the index in bytes.

MAIN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MISC.description:
For columns stored in persistent memory, displays the current memory
consumption not already accounted for in the other memory size columns in
bytes.

MAIN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_UNUSED.description:
Displays the amount of persistent memory, in bytes, that is not directly
mapped to a corresponding data structure during load, but was only read
to construct the data structure in DRAM. This column also includes
persistent memory block overhead if the requested persistent memory
allocation is not a multiple of a block size.

INDEX_CREATE_DURATION.description: Displays the time needed to create an inverted index.

DELTA_IMPLEMENTATION_FLAGS.description: Displays the internal column implementation specification summary.

INTERNAL_ATTRIBUTE_TYPE.description:
Displays the attribute type for internal columns. Returns NULL for
non-internal columns.

LAST_LOAD_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the column was loaded. This value is
undefined for unloaded columns.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays the persistent memory preference inferred from the
user-specified preferences.

NUMA_NODE_INDEX.description:
Displays the NUMA node index where allocations are performed and columns
are loaded.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_FILE_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the persistent memory file associated with the
column. If there is no associated persistent memory file, then NULL is
returned.

M_CS_ALL_COLUMN_STATISTICS.description: Provides information on how many scans and index searches were performed on any specified columns.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Returns the table partition ID:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.For replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original table and subsequent part IDs
are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table schema is currently in
progress.

SCANNED_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of scanned rows on the main part of a specified column. The value is
reset when the column is unloaded.

INDEX_LOOKUP_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of index lookups performed on the main part of a specified column.
The value is reset when the column is unloaded or merged.

M_CS_COLUMNS.description: Provides runtime information about columns in column tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Returns the table partition ID:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.For replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original table and
subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.The part ID -1 indicates that the table schema is being
modified.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description: Displays the sum, in bytes, of the memory used.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN.description:
Displays the current memory consumption, in bytes, in main. Returns 0 if
the memory is not loaded in main.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA.description:
Displays the current memory consumption, in bytes, in delta. Returns 0 if
the memory is not loaded in delta.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_PAGE_LOADABLE_MAIN.description: Displays the total paged memory size of the column in bytes.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description:
For columns stored in persistent memory, displays the total memory size
in bytes.

UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE.description:
Displays the estimated column size, in bytes, if the column is not
compressed.

COMPRESSION_RATIO_IN_PERCENTAGE.description:
Displays the compression ratio percentage. This value is determined by
dividing the MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL by the UNCOMPRESSED_SIZE and
multiplying the result by 100.

COUNT.description:
Displays the record count. This value is -1 if the count is not
loaded.

DISTINCT_COUNT.description:
Displays a distinct count of values, generally. This value is 0 if the
count is not loaded.The values in this column are not equal to actual values that might be
returned by a SELECT DISTINCT query. Do not use these values in a
production system if precision is required.

DISTINCT_COUNT_IN_MAIN.description: Displays the number of distinct values in MAIN. -1 if not loaded.

DISTINCT_COUNT_IN_DELTA.description: Displays the number of distinct values in DELTA. -1 if not loaded.

COMPRESSION_TYPE.description: Displays the type of column compression:SPARSEPREFIXEDCLUSTEREDINDIRECTRLEDEFAULT (if the column is only dictionary coded)

INDEX_TYPE.description: Displays the type of inverted index:FULLDisplays that a conventional index is defined on the column.BLOCKDisplays that a block index is defined on the column.MINMAXDisplays that a min-max index is defined on the column.NONEDisplays that no index is defined on the column.

INDEX_LOADED.description:
Displays the load status of the inverted index: NOT APPLICABLE, UNLOADED,
or LOADED.

IMPLEMENTATION_FLAGS.description:
Displays the internal implementation specification summary of the
column.

LAST_ACCESS_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the column was read or there was an INSERT
to, UPDATE of, or DELETE from the column. This value is undefined for
unloaded columns.

LOADED.description: Indicates whether the column is loaded into the memory: TRUE/FALSE.

LOADED_FROM_PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays whether the column was loaded from persistent memory:
TRUE/FALSE.

STORED_IN_PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays whether there is an associated persistent memory block:
TRUE/FALSE.

LOAD_UNIT.description: Displays the load unit for the column: PAGE, COLUMN, and UNKNOWN.

LAST_LOAD_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the column was loaded. This is undefined for
unloaded columns.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays the persistent memory preference inferred from the
user-specified preferences in a bottom-up manner starting from this
object: TRUE/FALSE.

NUMA_NODE_INDEX.description:
Displays the NUMA node index where allocations are performed and columns
are loaded.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_FILE_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the persistent memory file associated with the
column. If there is no associated persistent memory file, then NULL is
returned.

M_CS_COLUMNS_PERSISTENCE.description: Provides column persistence information for column tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Returns the table partition ID:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.For replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica
tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table schema is currently in
progress.

PERSISTENCE_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of column persistence: SINGLE, PAGED, UNIFIED, VIRTUAL_FILE, or
VIRTUAL_PAGED.

LOAD_UNIT.description:
Displays the unit of column data loading derived from user given preference: COLUMN or
PAGE. See3501427

MAIN_PHYSICAL_SIZE.description: Displays the storage size, in bytes, used by the column.

MAIN_PHYSICAL_SIZE_IN_PAGE_LOADABLE.description:
Displays the total on-disk size stored in a page-loadable format for the table in
bytes.

M_CS_INDEXES.description: Provides information for column store indexes.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number of the partition,
starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original table and
subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table schema is in
progress.

HASH_COLLISION_COUNT.description: Displays the count of hash collisions aggregated over all values.

CONCAT_COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the concat column in the case of multi-column indexes. This is empty
in the case of a single-column index.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description:
Displays the total size of the concat plus the sum of the
inverted index sizes of the single index columns in bytes.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_CONCAT.description: Displays the total size of the concat in bytes.

MOST_SELECTIVE_COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the most selective index column of a composite index. The most
selective column is the one with lowest cost value. NULL appears
if some of the index columns are not loaded.

INVERTED_INDIVIDUAL_COST.description:
Displays the cost value for INVERTED INDIVIDUAL index type. This cost value is an
indicator for the possible performance impact when switching
between different index-types. A high cost value indicates a
possible high overhead when using INVERTED INDIVIDUAL index-type
compared to INVERTED VALUE index-type. A cost value of 1
indicates that there is no overhead. For non composite or
non-unique indexes the value is -1, which indicates that no cost
value is available. The cost value is available only if all
index columns are loaded. A cost value of 0 indicates that some
index columns are currently not loaded. A manual load of the
index columns can be done using the LOAD Statement.

M_CS_LOADS.description: Provides a history of column loads.

LOAD_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp of the load event.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the column name. This is left empty if the whole table is
loaded.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

IS_HISTORY.description:
Displays the flag to indicate whether the history part is loaded (this is
only relevant for history tables). This is left empty if the whole table
is loaded.

LOAD_DURATION.description: Displays the load duration, in milliseconds.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the unique identifier for an SQL string.

M_CS_LOB_SPACE_RECLAIMS.description: Provides information regarding executed LOB garbage collection runs.

M_CS_LOB_SPACE_RECLAIM_DETAILS.description: Provides detailed statistics per LOB owner for LOB garbage collection runs.

M_CS_LOG_REPLAY_QUEUE_STATISTICS.description: Provides information about column store log replay queue statistics.

HOST.description: Displays the name of the host.

LOG_REPLAY_QUEUE_ID.description: Displays the ID of the recovery queue.

TOTAL_CS_LOG_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of redo records that were processed in
this queue.

TOTAL_DML_LOG_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of column store DML redo records that
were processed in this queue.

TOTAL_DDL_LOG_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of column store DDL redo records that
were processed in this queue.

LAST_CS_LOG_RECORD_TYPE.description: Displays the last column store log record type in this queue.

LAST_LOG_REPLAY_POSITION.description:
Displays the last log replay position that was processed in this
queue.

LAST_SAVEPOINT_POSITION.description:
Displays the last savepoint position that was passed in this
queue.

TOTAL_SAVEPOINT_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of savepoints that were passsed in this
queue.

ACTIVE_TABLE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of tables that are currently handled in this
queue.

ACTIVE_DELTA_MERGE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of delta merges that are currently ongoing
for tables that are handled in this queue.

TOTAL_DELTA_MERGE_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of delta merges that were started on
this queue.

TOTAL_SUCCESSFUL_DELTA_MERGE_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of successful merges.

TOTAL_DELTA_FUSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of delta fusions that were made in this
queue.

ACTIVE_OPTIMIZE_COMPRESSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of optimize compressions that are currently
ongoing for tables that are handled in this queue.

TOTAL_OPTIMIZE_COMPRESSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of successful optimize
compressions.

TOTAL_SUCCESSFUL_OPTIMIZE_COMPRESSION_CO.description:
Displays the total number of successful optimize
compressions.

TOTAL_DML_CONTEXT_CREATE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of expensive creations of DML contexts.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

IS_HISTORY.description: Indicates if the partition is a history partition: TRUE/FALSE.

LOADED.description: Indicates if the MVCC data is loaded into the memory.

FRAGMENT_TYPE.description: Displays the fragment type: DELTA1, DELTA2, or MAIN.

RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of visible records in the table.

RAW_RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of physical records in the table.

ROWSTATE_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of active rowstate blocks.

CTS_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of blocks used to store creation timestamps (CTS).

DTS_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of blocks used to store deletion timestamps (DTS).

ROWSTATE_STUB_BLOCK_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of stub blocks, both visible and invisible, present in the MVCC
layer associated with the fragment.

VISIBLE_ROWSTATE_STUB_BLOCK_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of visible stub blocks present in the MVCC layer associated with the
fragment.

CTS_STUB_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of active CTS stub blocks.

DTS_STUB_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of active DTS stub blocks.

FREE_ROWSTATE_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of free row-state blocks available for successive operations.

FREE_TS_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of free CTS/DTS blocks.

FREE_TS_STUB_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of free CTS/DTS stub blocks.

PAGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of pages in the MVCC page chain.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description:
If MVCC data is loaded, then it displays the total memory size of MVCC structures in
bytes.

PAGE_CHAIN_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
If MVCC data is loaded, then it displays the memory size of the pinned MVCC page chain in
bytes.

PAGE_CHAIN_ACTIVE_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size of the active part of the MVCC page chain in bytes.

ROWSTATE_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
If MVCC data is loaded, then it displays the memory size of transient rowstate structures
in bytes.

CTS_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the amount of memory used to store CTS blocks in bytes.

DTS_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the amount of memory used to store DTS blocks in bytes.

MISC_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
If MVCC data is loaded, then it displays the memory size of miscellaneous MVCC related
structures in bytes.

SPARSE_DTS_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of DTS blocks that are sparse timestamp blocks.

SPARSE_DTS_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size of the sparse block in bytes.

FREE_SPARSE_TS_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of available sparse timestamp blocks for successive operations.

M_CS_NSE_ADVISOR_DETAILS.description: Provides details of all objects included in the workload evaluated by the NSE advisor.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the object.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the table name of the object.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays object ID of the table.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. When the recommendation level is not PARTITION, this field is
not applicable and the value 0 is used.

COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the column name. When the recommendation level is not COLUMN, this field is not
applicable and the value NULL is used.

GRANULARITY.description:
Displays the object level of the entry. Possible values are: TABLE, PARTITION, or
COLUMN.

CONFIDENCE.description:
Displays a score (range [0-100]) that indicates the confidence of the recommendation. The
value can then be used to objectively compare recommendations of
a single NSE advisor run. It shows -1 if the object is not
advised.

CURRENT_LOAD_UNIT.description:
Displays the current load unit for this object. Possible values are: COLUMN, PAGE. If no
information is gathered, the value is an empty string.

TARGET_LOAD_UNIT.description:
Displays the recommended load unit for this object. Possible values are: COLUMN, PAGE. If
no recommendations are generated, the value is an empty
string.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN.description:
Displays the memory consumption size, in bytes, per granularity of the object in main
memory. If not LOADED or gathered, the value is 0 is used.

MAIN_PHYSICAL_SIZE.description: Displays the storage size, in bytes, per granularity of the object. If not gathered, the value 0 is used.

SKIP_REASON.description:
Displays the reason why this object is skipped and not
advised.

AUTO_APPLY_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when AUTO_APPLY_STATUS was last set.

AUTO_APPLY_STATUS.description:
Displays the status of auto apply. Values are: NONE, PENDING,
SUCCEED, FAILED, INVALID or CANCELLED.
