REQUEST_ID.description: Displays an increasing index of requests. Start from 1 and will loop back from 1 when overflow.

ALGORITHM.description: Displays algorithm name. Possible values are: RULE, SMART.

START_TIME.description: Displays start time of run of NSE Advisor.

END_TIME.description: Displays end time of run of NSE advisor. If this run has not finished, it will be NULL.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays connection ID that initializes run of NSE advisor.

CURRENT_PROGRESS.description:
Displays estimated progress of the current run.(0,100) indicates it is waiting
for duration and calculation step. (0,99) indicates that the
recommendations calculation is in progress. 100 means that
the recommendations calculation has finished. (100, 200)
indicates auto apply is in progress. 200 indicates all jobs
have finished.If any internal errors occur, this
estimated progress doesn't change. Then it can be used to
determine in which progress the error occurred.

MAX_PROGRESS.description:
Displays the maximum operation progress of the current run. Value is always
200.

RECOMMENDATION_COUNT.description: Displays how many recommendations are produced in the run.

DURATION.description: Displays duration used in the run.

PROGRESS_DETAIL.description: Displays Additional information for the run.

M_CS_TABLE_HANDLES.description: Shows the threads waiting for table locks.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name as used in SQL.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the table name as used in SQL.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the unique internal table ID which remains unchanged during rename.

PART_ID.description: Displays the logical partition number, 0 if the table is not partitioned, -1 if the logical partition number may not be retrieved from metadata.

INTERNAL_PART_ID.description: Displays the internal partition number as used in internal IndexName objects.

LOCK_ID.description: Displays the unique identifier for the table at IndexMgr level, also found in dumps, trace output, etc.

HANDLE_ID.description: Displays the unique memory address of the IndexHandle.

THREAD_ID.description: Displays the thread using the IndexHandle (when it was acquired).

CURRENT_STATE.description: Displays the current state of the IndexHandle. Values are: search_delta, finish_delta_merge, delete., etc. It is the lowercase state name with the prefix ns_ removed.

NEXT_STATE.description: Displays the next state of the IndexHandle when waiting for a lock upgrade. Values are the same as for CURRENT_STATE.

INITIAL_ACQUIRE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp value when IndexHandles are upgraded or downgraded to different states. This preserves the time when the IndexHandle was first acquired along with the table name.

ACQUIRE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp value for the acquired time.

QUEUE_POSITION.description: Displays the position within the queue, when waiting; otherwise the value is 0.

M_CS_TABLE_LOCKS.description: Shows the locked tables threads waiting to lock tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name as used in SQL.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the table name as used in SQL.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the unique internal table ID, which remains unchanged during rename.

PART_ID.description: Displays the logical partition number, 0 if the table is not partitioned, -1 if the logical partition number cannot be retrieved from metadata.

INTERNAL_PART_ID.description: Displays the internal partition number as used in internal IndexName objects.

ACQUIRED_HANDLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of handles acquired for the table.

WAITING_HANDLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of handles waiting for access to the table.

LOCK_ID.description: Displays the unique identifier for the table at IndexMgr level, also found in dumps, trace output, etc.

IS_COORDINATOR.description: Displays the internal flag used for asserting in IndexMgr code.

IS_SHARED.description: Displays the internal flag used for asserting in IndexMgr code.

IS_BLOCK_MAIN.description: I Displays the iternal flag used for asserting in IndexMgr code.

IS_TABLE.description: Displays the internal flag used for asserting in IndexMgr code.

IS_MODE_NO_WAIT.description: Displays the IS_MODE_NO_WAIT flag. Access to all current handles is granted without waiting in an optimized per thread structure since none of the active handles are in conflict. This internal flag is automatically set and reset during locking operations to trigger internal performance optimizations, it does not relate to the NOWAIT keyword of SQL.

M_CS_UNLOADS.description: Provides a history of column unloads.

UNLOAD_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp of unload event.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the column name. Empty if the whole table is unloaded.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

IS_HISTORY.description:
Indicates whether the history part is unloaded (only relevant for history
tables). Empty if the whole table is unloaded.

REASON.description:
Displays the possible reasons for the unload. Values are LOW MEMORY,
SHRINK, EXPLICIT, MERGE, or UNUSED RESOURCE. Unloads caused by a manual
shrink are reported as SHRINK, whereas unloads caused by memory
management's automatic shrink on out of memory (OOM) are reported as LOW
MEMORY.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays whether persistent memory is removed as part of the unload.
Values are RETAIN or DELETE. Empty if the user did not specify a value
for the unload.

M_CUSTOMIZABLE_FUNCTIONALITIES.description: Provides information about the enablement status of restricted features in databases. The full content of this view is only accessible from the system database.

IS_ENABLED.description: Indicates whether the customizable functionality is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

START_TIME.description: Displays the start time.

USAGE.description:
Database usage type. Recommended values: production, test, development,
or custom. Any value is allowed, but recommended values may be used by
clients to alter behavior.

M_DATABASES.description: Provides information about all databases in the system. The full content of this view is only accessible from the system database.

ACTIVE_STATUS_DETAILS.description:
Displays the database status details. Possible values include: stopped by user, stopped
due to broken recovery, or stopped by takeover.

OS_USER.description: Displays the database isolation operation system user.

OS_GROUP.description: Displays the database isolation operation system group.

RESTART_MODE.description:
Displays the restart behavior after a system restart. Possible values are NO (do not
restart the database after a system restart) or DEFAULT (restore
the database to the state it had before the system restart).

FALLBACK_SNAPSHOT_CREATE_TIME.description:
Displays the timestamp for when the fallback snapshot was created, or NULL if there is no
fallback snapshot for the database. The value for this column is
always NULL for SYSTEMDB.

INSTALL_TIME.description: Displays the installation or first start time.

M_DATABASE_REPLICAS.description: Provides source and target information for databases involved in replication.

SOURCE_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the source database.

SOURCE_SYSTEMDB_HOST.description: Displays the host of the source system database.

SOURCE_SYSTEMDB_PORT.description: Displays the source system database port.

TARGET_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the target database.

TARGET_SYSTEMDB_HOST.description: Displays the target system database host.

TARGET_SYSTEMDB_PORT.description: Displays the target system database port.

REPLICATION_STATUS.description: Displays the aggregated replication status of the database services.

M_DATABASE_REPLICA_STATISTICS.description: Provides statistics on databases involved in replication.

SOURCE_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the source database.

TARGET_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the target database.

TARGET_CONNECT_TIME.description: Displays the time that the connection was established from the target.

TARGET_FULLY_RECOVERABLE.description: Indicates if the target is fully recoverable.

LAST_LOG_POSITION_TIME.description: Displays the current log position timestamp.

SHIPPED_LOG_POSITION_TIME.description: Displays the shipped log position timestamp.

SHIPPED_LOG_BUFFERS_COUNT.description: Displays the shipped log buffers count.

SHIPPED_LOG_BUFFERS_SIZE.description: Displays the shipped log buffers size in bytes.

SHIPPED_LOG_BUFFERS_DURATION.description: Displays the shipped log buffer duration in microseconds.

ASYNC_BUFFER_FULL_COUNT.description: Displays the number of times the asynchronous replication buffer got full.

BACKLOG_SIZE.description: Displays the current replication backlog in bytes.

MAX_BACKLOG_SIZE.description: Displays the max replication backlog in bytes.

BACKLOG_TIME.description: Displays the current replication backlog in microseconds.

MAX_BACKLOG_TIME.description: Displays the max replication backlog in microseconds.

M_DATA_STATISTICS.description: Lists data statistics generated when you query column and row store object.

DATA_STATISTICS_OID.description: Displays the data statistics object ID.

DATA_STATISTICS_TYPE.description: Displays the data statistics object type.

DATA_STATISTICS_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the data statistics object schema.

DATA_STATISTICS_NAME.description: Displays the data statistics object name.

DATA_SOURCE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the data source object.

DATA_SOURCE_OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the data source object.

DATA_SOURCE_COLUMN_NAMES.description: Lists the column names of the data source.

DATA_SOURCE_STORAGE_TYPE.description: Displays the source storage type of the data source.

DATA_SOURCE_PART_ID.description: Displays the partition ID of the data source.

LAST_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the time the data statistics on the object were last generated.

LAST_REFRESH_REASON.description: Displays the time the data statistics object was generated.

DATA_STATISTICS_CONTENT.description:
Lists the content and properties of the data statistics object as of the last refresh
(JSON format). The content section is empty for SIMPLE, SKETCH,
and RECORD COUNT; it contains bucket boundaries and counts for
HISTOGRAM and a values and frequencies list for TOPK. For
SAMPLE, it contains a list of sample output. The content is
similar to exported data for data statistics objects.The properties section contains properties relevant to the data statistics of the
specified type. The properties section, when present, may
include some of the following properties:MEMORYDisplays the memory size used by the data statistics object in bytesMEMORY PERCENTDisplays the memory size used by the data statistics object, as a percentage of the
data source sizeCOUNTDisplays the number of rows in the data sourceDISTINCT COUNTDisplays the number of unique values in the data sourceNULL COUNTDisplays the number of NULL values in the data sourceBUCKETSDisplays the number of buckets in the data statistics objectQERRORDisplays the value of the qerror parameter used for building the data statistics
objectQTHETADisplays the value of the qtheta parameter used for building the data statistics
objectACCURACYDisplays the accuracy parameter used for building the data statistics objectPREFIX BITSDisplays the prefix bits parameter used for building the data statistics objectMIN VALUEDisplays the minimum value in the data source in bytesMAX VALUEDisplays the maximum value in the data source in bytesMAX_ROWIDDisplays the maximum rowID for the data statistics objectMIN MAX IS VALIDDisplays whether MIN/MAX values are validSAMPLE SIZEDisplays the sample size at build time, expressed in bytesSAMPLE SIZE PERCENTDisplays the sample size at build time, expressed as a percent of the data volume

PARTITION_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

FILE_NAME.description: Displays the filename of the data volume.

FILE_ID.description: Displays the file ID of data volume.

STATE.description: Displays the data volume state: ACTIVATING, ACTIVE, or DEACTIVATING.

SIZE.description: Displays the size of the data volume in bytes.

MAX_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum size of the data volume in bytes.

M_DATA_VOLUME_PAGE_STATISTICS.description: Provides page usage statistics on data volumes.

PARTITION_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

STATE.description: Displays the data volume state: ACTIVATING, ACTIVE, or DEACTIVATING.

PAGE_SIZE.description: Displays the page size in bytes.

SUPERBLOCK_SIZE.description: Displays the superblock size in bytes.

INITIAL_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of init pages.

TOTAL_ALLOCATE_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of single and group allocated blocks.

TOTAL_SET_BLOCK_FREE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of single and group freed blocks.

TOTAL_SET_BLOCK_FREE_AFTER_SAVEPOINT_COU.description: Displays the number of single and group freed-after-savepoint blocks.

SUPERBLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of used superblocks.

USED_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of used blocks.

SHADOW_BLOCK_COUNT.description: Displays the number of shadow blocks.

PARTITION_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

FILE_NAME_PATTERN.description: Displays the file name pattern for the data volume partition file.

STATE.description:
Displays the data volume state. This can be specified as ACTIVATING or ACTIVE. When a
data volume partition is added, it is in the ACTIVATING state
and is not usable until the next savepoint, at which time it
enters the ACTIVE state.

MAX_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the maximum file size for the data volume files in bytes. A value of 0 means
that there is no limitation for the file size.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used size of the data volume partition in bytes.

TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the total size of the data volume partition in bytes.

FILES_SIZE.description: Displays the size of the data volume files on the disk in bytes.

FILL_RATIO.description: Displays the fill ratio of the data volume partition.

M_DATA_VOLUME_RECLAIM_STATISTICS.description: Displays statistical information on reclamation operations on a data volume.

DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the database.

HOST.description: Displays the name of the Host.

STATISTICS_ID.description: Displays the statistics object unique ID.

START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the reclaim.

END_TIME.description: Displays the end time of the reclaim.

DURATION.description: Displays the duration of the reclaim.

RECLAIM_THRESHOLD.description:
Specifies the desired percentage of the payload to which the
data volume should be reduced.

STEP_COUNT.description: Displays the number of reclaimed steps already performed.

RECLAIMED_PARTITION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of successfully reclaimed or currently
reclaiming partitions.

FAILED_RECLAIM_PARTITION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of partitions on which reclaim operation
failed.

PARTITION_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of data volume partitions.

INITIAL_USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used size in bytes before the reclaim.

INITIAL_TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the total size in bytes before the reclaim.

INITIAL_FILE_SIZE.description: Displays the file's size in bytes before the reclaim.

INITIAL_FILL_RATIO.description: Displays the fill ratio before reclaim.

RECLAIM_USED_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size in bytes during the reclaim or right
after it.

RECLAIM_TOTAL_SIZE.description:
Displays the total size in bytes during the reclaim or right
after it.

RECLAIM_FILE_SIZE.description: Displays the file's size during reclaim or right after it

RECLAIM_FILL_RATIO.description:
Displays the fill ratio during the reclaim or right after
it.

TOTAL_MOVED_SIZE.description: Displays the total size in bytes of moved data.

TOTAL_MOVED_PAGES.description: Displays the total number of moved pages.

TOTAL_MOVE_TIME.description: Displays the total time in microseconds for moving pages.

TOTAL_TRUNCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the total size in bytes of truncated data.

TOTAL_TRUNCATE_TIME.description: Displays the total time in microseconds for truncating data.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user.

M_DATA_VOLUME_STATISTICS.description: Provides information on data volume statistics.

PATH_COUNT.description: Displays the number of partition paths.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used size of the data volume in bytes.

TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the total size of the data volume in bytes.

FILL_RATIO.description: Displays the fill ratio of the data volume.

M_DEBUG_CONNECTIONS.description: Provides an overview of connections used per debug session.

DEBUG_SESSION_ID.description: Displays the landscape-wide unique identifier for debug session.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection that is used for communication.

CONNECTION_USAGE.description:
Indicates whether this connection is used for debugger communication or
debugging.

OPERATION.description:
Displays the operation that is currently running within this
connection.

M_DEBUG_SESSIONS.description: Provides an overview of debug sessions and their properties.

DEBUG_SESSION_ID.description: Displays the landscape-wide unique identifier for debug session.

COMPILE_MODE.description: Displays the compilation handling of nested procedures.

TIMEOUT.description:
Displays the time in seconds after which the debug session will timeout
and destroy itself.

ATTACH_COUNT.description: Displays the number of debuggees the debugger is currently attached to.

ATTACH_FILTER_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID the debugger is using to attach to
connections.

ATTACH_FILTER_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the connection user name the debugger is using to attach to
connections.

ATTACH_FILTER_APPLICATION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the application user name the debugger is using to attach to
connections.

ATTACH_FILTER_DEBUG_TOKEN.description:
Displays the debug token the debugger is using to attach to
connections.

M_DELTA_MERGE_STATISTICS.description: Provides information on table delta merge statistics.

TYPE.description: Displays the type of the statistic:MERGEThe table delta merge.HINTThe application merge hint.SPARSEOptimizes compression.FACTThe fact table compression.RECLAIMThe table delta garbage collection.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

HISTORY.description: Displays TRUE when the history part was merged. Otherwise FALSE.

MEMORY_MERGE.description:
Deprecated. Use the Type column, which displays more detailed
information.

PASSPORT.description:
Displays the external identifier for the table merge called by an
application.

LOG_REPLAY_QUEUE_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the log replay queue where the job was started. During
log replay, only delta merge and optimize compression operations are
possible.

START_TIME.description: Displays the execution start time.

RESOURCE_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the total wait time in milliseconds for memory and CPU resources
when too many merges or optimized compressions have been started in
parallel.

PHASE_1_HESITANT_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the total wait time in milliseconds to acquire an exclusive lock
in the first exclusive merge phase. The time includes configured timeout
and subsequent retries.

PHASE_1_BLOCKING_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the wait time in milliseconds to acquire an exclusive lock in
the first exclusive merge phase in the event the hesitant acquire was
unsuccessful. New readers and writers are already blocked.

PHASE_1_LOCK_TIME.description:
Displays the time in milliseconds spent under exclusive lock in the first
exclusive merge phase.

PHASE_2_HESITANT_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the total wait time in milliseconds to acquire an exclusive lock
for the second exclusive merge phase or the exclusive optimize
compression phase, respectively. The time includes configured timeout and
subsequent retries."

PHASE_2_BLOCKING_LOCK_WAIT_TIME.description:
Displays the time in milliseconds to acquire an exclusive lock for the
second exclusive merge phase or the exclusive optimize compression phase,
respectively, in the event the hesitant acquire was unsuccessful. New
readers and writers are already blocked.

PHASE_2_LOCK_TIME.description:
Displays the time in milliseconds spent under exclusive lock in the
second exclusive merge phase or the exclusive optimize compression phase,
respectively.

EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the execution duration in milliseconds.

MOTIVATION.description:
Displays the motivation of the statistics:AUTOTriggered based on an automatic decision function.SMARTTriggered by a HINT from the user based on a smart decision
function.CRITICALTriggered based on a critical decision function.HARDTriggered via SQL based on a hard decision function.FORCEDA merge triggered via SQL, circumventing resource
availability checks, based on a forced decision function.

SUCCESS.description:
Displays a call success flag, which depends on the field TYPE.HINTDisplays whether the application merge hint was accepted or
rejected.MERGE/SPARSEDisplays whether the delta merge/optimize compression was
completed with or without success: TRUE/FALSE. For example, a
table delta merge call which did not result in a delta merge
because the delta was empty, is indicated with FALSE.

OLD_MAIN_RECORDS.description:
Displays the number of rows in old main prior to the delta merge. -1
indicates other operation types.

MERGED_MAIN_RECORDS.description:
Displays the number of rows merged from old main to new main. -1
indicates other operation types.

OLD_DELTA_RECORDS.description:
Displays the number of rows merged from old main to new main. -1
indicates other operation types.

MERGED_DELTA_RECORDS.description:
Displays the number of rows merged from old delta1 to new main for MERGE
operation or the number of rows evicted from delta during RECLAIM delta
operation. -1 indicates other operation types.

NEW_MAIN_RECORDS.description:
Displays the total number of rows in new main after the merge. It is the
sum of MERGED_MAIN_RECORDS and MERGED_DELTA_RECORDS. -1 indicates other
operation types.

OLD_MAIN_IN_USE.description:
Displays TRUE when old main is still in use by old readers once the merge
or optimize compression is finished. Old main will only be deleted after
the last reader has finished. FALSE indicates other operation types.

OLD_DELTA_IN_USE.description:
Displays TRUE when old delta1 is still in use by old readers once the
merge is finished. It will only be deleted after the last reader has
finished. FALSE indicates other operation types.

LAST_ERROR.description:
Displays the error code of the last error that occurred. This explains
why a merge did not succeed. See ERROR_DESCRIPTION for details.

CS_ERROR.description:
Displays the column store specific error code. See ERROR_DESCRIPTION for
details.

ERROR_DESCRIPTION.description:
Displays the description of the last error that occurred during the
merge. A failing merge does not necessarily indicate a problem.

M_DISKS.description: Provides information about disk configuration and utilization of the host machine.

DEVICE_ID.description: Displays the database internal device ID.

HOST.description:
Displays the host name. This is only set if the disk is used by exactly
one host.

USAGE_TYPE.description:
Displays the usage type. Values are: LOG, DATA, TRACE, DATA_BACKUP,
LOG_BACKUP, CATALOG_BACKUP,, ROOTKEY_BACKUP, or XSA_WORKSPACE.

TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the usable total size of the device in bytes.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used size of the device in bytes.

TOTAL_DEVICE_SIZE.description:
Displays the total device size returned by the operating system in
bytes.

MOUNT_SOURCE.description: Displays the source of the mount.

MOUNT_DETAILS.description: Displays the mount details as stated in/proc/mount.

M_DISK_USAGE.description: Provides disk usage information on host basis group by resource types.

USAGE_TYPE.description:
Displays the resource type: LOG, DATA, TRACE, LOG_BACKUP or
DATA_BACKUP.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the size of the used disk space in bytes.

M_DYNAMIC_RESULT_CACHE.description: Lists statistics for the dynamic result cache.

CACHE_ID.description: Displays the unique ID for each dynamic result cache entry.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name that the dynamic result cache entry belongs to.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name that the dynamic result cache entry belongs to.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type that the dynamic result cache entry belongs to.

DETAILS.description: Displays the details of all of the constituents of the cache key.

LAST_MVCC_SNAPSHOT_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the last cached MVCC snapshot timestamp.

MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the size, in bytes, of the memory occupied by the dynamic result cache
entry.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_INDEX.description:
Displays the size, in bytes, of the memory occupied by the dynamic result cache entry's
indexes.

RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of records in the cache entry.

DELTA_RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of records in the cache entry created by a delta refresh.

IS_REFRESHING.description:
Displays the status of the asynchronous cache entry refresh job. The result is TRUE if
the refresh job is currently running and FALSE otherwise.

CURRENT_REFRESH_DURATION.description:
Displays the duration, in milliseconds, of the currently running cache entry refresh job.
The value is 1 when IS_REFRESHING is FALSE.

CURRENT_REFRESH_REASON.description:
Displays the reason why the refresh job was triggered. The value is empty when
IS_REFRESHING is FALSE.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the dynamic result cache entry was created.

LAST_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the last cache entry refresh job finished.

LAST_REFRESH_DURATION.description: Displays the duration, in milliseconds, of the last cache entry refresh job.

LAST_REFRESH_REASON.description: Displays the reason why the last refresh job was triggered.

LAST_DELTA_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the last delta refresh on the cache entry finished.

LAST_ACCESS_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp of the last cache entry access.

REFRESH_COUNT.description: Displays the number of refreshes on the cache entry.

DELTA_REFRESH_COUNT.description: Displays the number of delta refreshes on the cache entry.

ACCESS_COUNT.description: Displays the number of read accesses on the cache entry.

MISS_COUNT.description: Displays the number of cache miss read accesses on the cache entry.

M_DYNAMIC_RESULT_CACHE_EXCLUSIONS.description: Lists cache exclusions of the dynamic result cache.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name that the dynamic result cache entry belongs to.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name that the dynamic result cache entry belongs to.

DETAILS.description: Lists all constituents of the excluded cache key.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the size, in bytes, of the memory occupied by the excluded cache entry.

RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of records in the excluded cache entry.

EXCLUDE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the dynamic result cache entry was decided.

M_EFFECTIVE_PASSWORD_POLICY.description: Provides information about password policy parameters for database users.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user that this password policy is valid for.

VALUE.description: Displays the value of the password policy parameter.

M_EFFECTIVE_TABLE_PLACEMENT.description: Provides information about effective placement of tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema.

LOCATION_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the possible
location.

MIN_ROWS_FOR_PARTITIONING.description: Displays the minimum row count for partitioning.

MIN_ROWS_FOR_PARTITIONING_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the minimum row
count for partitioning.

PARTITIONING_THRESHOLD_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the repartitioning
threshold.

INITIAL_PARTITION_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the initial
partitions.

DYNAMIC_RANGE_THRESHOLD_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the dynamic range
threshold.

PAGE_LOADABLE.description: Displays the Data Page Loadable setting.

PAGE_LOADABLE_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the page loadable
flag.

REPLICA_COUNT_MATCH.description: Displays a string representing the matching rule for the replica count.

REPLICA_TYPE_MATCH.description:
Displays a string representing the matching rule for the replica
type.

M_ENCRYPTION_OVERVIEW.description: Reports the encryption status for all data at rest where encryption is supported.

SCOPE.description:
Displays whether the scope includes PERSISTENCE (data volumes), LOG (redo log), or
BACKUP.

IS_ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE.description: Indicates if the encryption for the scope is currently active: TRUE/FALSE.

LAST_CHANGE_TIME.description:
Displays the latest timestamp, in the server's local time, when the status was
changed

CONFIGURATION_CONTROL.description:
Indicates whether encryption configuration is controlled by the local database or the
system database.

ENCRYPTION_CONTROL_LAST_CHANGE_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the tenant encryption control was
changed.

M_EPM_SESSIONS.description: Provides all EPM sessions with detailed information.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

STATUS.description: Displays the status.

M_ES_DELTA_MEMORY.description: Provides extended storage DELTA memory information.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

FRAGMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of store fragments for the table.

TOTAL_SIZE.description:
Displays the total DELTA memory used, in megabytes, by this
table.

DATA_SIZE.description:
Displays the total DELTA memory used, in megabytes, to store the
table data.

DICTIONARY_SIZE.description:
Displays the total DELTA memory used, in megabytes, to store the
dictionaries for this table.

BITMAP_SIZE.description:
Displays the DELTA memory used, in megabytes, to store the
table-level bitmaps.

RIDSPACE_INDEX.description: Displays the size of the RID space index in megabytes.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the associated transaction ID.

M_ES_DELTA_MERGE_STATISTICS.description: Provides extended storage delta merge statistics information.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

START_TIME.description: Displays the time that the merge started.

END_TIME.description: Displays the time that the merge ended.

STATUS.description:
Displays the status of the merge operation: STARTED, COMPLETED,
or FAILED.

ERROR_CODE.description: Displays the SQL code of the merge once completed.

MOTIVATION.description:
Displays the cause of the merge trigger: DML, DDL, SHUTDOWN, or
USER.

IS_BLOCKING.description: Indicates whether or not the merge was blocking: TRUE/FALSE.

INSERT_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that were inserted as a result of the
merge.

UPDATE_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that were updated as a result of the
merge.

DELETE_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that were deleted as a result of the
merge.

UNCOMMITTED_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that were uncommitted at the time of
the merge.

M_ES_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS.description: Provides extended storage expensive statement information.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction ID.

OPERATION.description: Displays the type of operation: PREPARE, EXECUTE, FETCH, CLOSE, or ES OPERATION.

START_TIME.description: Displays the statement start time.

DURATION_MICROSEC.description: Displays the statement duration in microseconds.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID that has the lock.

USER_ID.description: Displays the ID of the user who has the lock.

TABLE_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of table: BASE TABLE, GLOBAL TEMPORARY TABLE,
or MATERIALIZED VIEW.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the table.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table on which the lock is held.

INDEX_ID.description: Displays the index ID or NULL if there is no index ID.

LOCK_CLASS.description: Displays the lock class: SCHEMA, ROW, TABLE, or POSITION.

LOCK_DURATION_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of lock duration: TRANSACTION, POSITION, or
CONNECTION.

LOCK_TYPE.description:
Displays the lock type:For SCHEMA, it could be SHARED or EXCLUSIVEFor ROW, it could be: INTENT, READPK, WRITE, WRITTENOPK, or
SURROGATEFor TABLE, it could be: SHARED, INTENT, or EXCLUSIVEFor POSITION, it could be PHANTOM or INSERT

ROW_INDENTIFIER.description:
Displays the identifier for the row that the lock starts on. This
value is NULL if there is no lock.

ROW_RANGE.description:
Displays the number of contiguous rows that are locked. Row locks
in the DELTA store can either be a single row or a range of
rows.

CATEGORY.description:
Displays the main category type for which the parameters and
values are displayed: GENERAL, BUFFERS, MEMORY, STORAGE, DELTA,
BACKUP, and VERSIONING.

NAME.description:
Displays the name of the parameter provided in the VALUE
column.

VALUE.description: Displays the value of the parameter.

M_ES_RESULT_CACHE.description: Provides details about each result that has been cached. You must have the EXTENDED STORAGE ADMIN privilege to access this view.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table.

COLUMNS.description: Displays the columns included in the result as a comma-separated list.

PREDICATES.description: Displays the predicates used to generate the result as a comma-separated list.

COMMIT_ID.description: Displays the version number of the table.

STATEMENT_STRING.description: Displays the query that generated the result.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the result cache entry was generated and stored.

CREATE_DURATION.description: Displays the number of milliseconds it took to generate the original result.

CREATE_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID that generated the result.

LAST_ACCESS_TIME.description: Displays the last time that the result cache entry was accessed by a user.

LAST_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the last connection ID that accessed the result.

HIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of accesses on the cache entry.

SUBSET_HIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of cache entry accesses that only used a subset of the result.

EXACT_MATCH_HIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of cache entry accesses that used the whole result.

RESULT_SIZE.description: Displays the size of the memory occupied by the result cache entry in bytes.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction ID.

INTERNAL_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the 10-digit connection ID included as a part of all
messages in theesserver.trcfile.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the application.

USER_ID.description: Displays the user ID for the connection.

COMMIT_ID.description:
Displays the ID assigned by the transaction manager when the
transaction commits. For active transactions, the commit ID is
0.

VERSION_ID.description:
Displays the version ID. A value of 0 indicates that the
transaction has no version and that the ID has not been
assigned.

TRANSACTION_STATE.description:
Displays the state of the transaction: NONE, ACTIVE,
ROLLING_BACK, ROLLED_BACK, COMMITTING, COMMITTED, or
APPLIED.

MAIN_TABLE_USED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of ES_USER space created by the transaction
in megabytes.

MAIN_TABLE_FREED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of ES_USER space dropped by the transaction
in megabytes.

TEMP_TABLE_USED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of ES_TEMP space created by the transaction
in megabytes.

TEMP_TABLE_FREED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of ES_TEMP space dropped by the transaction
in megabytes.

TEMP_WORKSPACE_SIZE.description:
For ACTIVE transactions, displays a snapshot of the work space in
use at this instant by this transaction, such as sorts, hashes,
and temporary bitmaps in megabytes. This number changes
depending on the usage as memory is allocated and freed. When
the transaction is no longer active, this column is 0.

TRANSACTION_CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the transaction began.

CURSOR_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of open dynamic tiering cursors that
reference the transaction control block. If the transaction is
ACTIVE, it displays the number of open cursors created within
the transaction. If the transaction is COMMITTED, it displays
the number of hold cursors that reference a database version
owned by the transaction.

INTERNAL_SAVEPOINT_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of savepoint structures that exist within the
transaction control block.

INTERNAL_SAVEPOINT_NUMBER.description: Displays the active savepoint number of the transaction.

GLOBAL_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the global transaction ID associated with the
transaction. If there is no transaction, then this value is
0.

VERSIONING_TYPE.description:
Displays the snapshot versioning transaction type, either
table-level (default), or row-level. Row-level snapshot
versioning (DELTA) applies only to DELTA-enabled tables. Once a
transaction is started, this value cannot change.

IS_BLOCKED.description:
Indicates whether or not connection blocking is enabled:
TRUE/FALSE. If TRUE, the transaction blocks and waits for a
conflicting lock to release before it attempts to retry the lock
request.

BLOCKING_TIME_OUT.description:
Displays the time, in milliseconds, that a transaction waits for
a locking conflict to clear. A value of 0 (default) indicates
that the transaction waits indefinitely.

SOURCE_HOST.description: Displays the original host if the event was created via another host.

SOURCE_PORT.description: Displays the original port if the event was created via another host.

TYPE.description: Displays the type of event.

ID.description: Displays the ID of the event.

INFOTEXT.description: Displays additional information in free text.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the event was created.

UPDATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the event was updated.

HANDLE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the event was handled.

STATE.description: Displays the state of the event.

ACKNOWLEDGED.description: Indicates whether the event is acknowledged: TRUE/FALSE.

FAILED_HANDLES.description: Displays the number of failed handle attempts.

M_EXECUTED_STATEMENTS.description: Provides all statement executions that belong to a specified category.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction object ID.

UPDATE_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the write transaction ID. This number is ever
increasing.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the unique identifier for an SQL string.

SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the schema in whose context the statement is
executed.

CLIENT_IP.description: Displays the IP address of the client machine.

START_TIME.description: Displays the statement start time.

DURATION_MICROSEC.description: Displays the statement duration in microseconds.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the related object.

PASSPORT_ROOTCONTEXT_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) GUID that identifies the source of the request.

PASSPORT_TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) GUID that identifies the business transaction.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) GUID that identifies the connection.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_COUNTER.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) connection counter.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_NAME.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component name.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component type.

PASSPORT_ACTION.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component action.

PASSPORT_ACTION_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component action type.

PASSPORT_PREVIOUS_COMPONENT_NAME.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) passport component name of
the previous context.

PASSPORT_USER_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) user ID.

PASSPORT_TRACE_FLAGS.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) trace flags.

APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays the application that defines from which source file SAP
HANA is called. The usage is up to the application. This value
is also displayed in
M_PREPARED_STATEMENTS.APPLICATION_SOURCE.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the application.

M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS.description: Provides all statements with a duration longer than a specified threshold.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction object ID.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the unique identifier for an SQL string.

SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the schema in whose context the statement is
executed.

START_TIME.description: Displays the statement start time.

DURATION_MICROSEC.description: Displays the statement duration in microseconds.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the related objects.

OPERATION.description: Displays the type of operation (prepare, execute, fetch, or close).

LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated lock wait count.

LOCK_WAIT_DURATION.description: Displays the accumulated lock wait duration in microseconds.

MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory usage in bytes during the execution of the
statement. In cases where a query was executed in a scale-out system
(i.e. the query was running distributed across multiple hosts), then
MEMORY_SIZE is the highest peak memory usage across all involved hosts.
So if host A had a peak usage of 2 GB and host B has a peak usage of 3
GB, then MEMORY_SIZE displays 3 GB.This value is kept for cache reasons or released after the statement
execution.

REUSED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the memory that is reused from cached data structures in bytes.
The value is 0 if the cache is empty.

CPU_TIME.description:
Displays the CPU time, in microseconds, consumed to compute the
statement.

PASSPORT_ROOTCONTEXT_ID.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) GUID that identifies the source of
the request.

PASSPORT_TRANSACTION_ID.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) GUID that identifies the business
transaction.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) GUID that identifies the
connection.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_COUNTER.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) connection counter.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_NAME.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component name.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component type.

PASSPORT_ACTION.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component action.

PASSPORT_ACTION_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component action type.

PASSPORT_PREVIOUS_COMPONENT_NAME.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) passport component name of the
previous context.

PASSPORT_USER_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) user ID.

PASSPORT_TRACE_FLAGS.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) trace flags.

STATEMENT_START_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the first operation of a statement execution
starts. All operations of a statement execution (prepare, execute, fetch,
or close) share the same statement start time.

APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays the application from which source file SAP HANA is called. Usage
is determined by the application. The value is also displayed in
M_PREPARED_STATEMENTS.APPLICATION_SOURCE.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the application.

NETWORK_MESSAGE_ID.description:
Displays the client message ID on the physical server connection, also
found in M_SQL_CLIENT_NETWORK_IO and MESSAGE_ID.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the effective workload class.

STATEMENT_THREAD_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective statement thread limit.

STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective statement memory limit.

TOTAL_STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective total statement memory limit.

TOTAL_STATEMENT_THREAD_LIMIT.description:
Displays the maximum number of parallel threads all statements may
execute on.

M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENT_EXECUTION_LOCATION.description: Provides location statistics for expensive statements.

STATEMENT_EXECUTION_ID.description: Displays the execution ID of the statement.

START_TIME.description: Displays the execution start time of the statement.

EXECUTION_HOST.description: Displays the host where the statement was executed.

EXECUTION_PORT.description: Displays the port of the host where the statement was executed.

MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory usage for the host in bytes. For example, if host A has a peak
usage of 2 GB and host B has a peak usage of 3 GB, then there is
a row with host A showing 2 GB and a row with host B showing 3
GB.

CPU_TIME.description: Displays the tracked CPU time that each location consumed.

M_EXPORT_BINARY_STATUS.description: Provides export status information for the current session.

CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID to distinguish the data of each export session
(-1 for Python).

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the table or view being exported.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table or view being exported.

INDEX_TYPE.description:
Displays the index type: UNKNOWN, PHYSICAL, OLAP, JOIN, HIERARCHY, or
CALCULATION.

STATUS.description:
Displays the export status: QUEUED, WORKING, SKIPPED, DONE, or
FAILED.

ERROR.description:
Displays empty if the export is successful. If the export was
unsuccessful, then this row displays error text.

M_EXTRACTORS.description: Provides direct extractor connection (DXC) status information

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the schema where the DSO tables are generated.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the DSO-specific status table.

CLIENT.description: Displays the ID of the client.

STATUS.description: Displays the general status overview: OK/ERROR.

REQUEST_ID.description: Displays the SID of the request used for activation.

ERROR_MESSAGE.description: Displays the message in case of an error.

PHASE.description: Displays the phase of the last running operation.

TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the timestamp of the operation phase.

M_FEATURES.description: Provides information about all supported features.

COMPONENT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the component owning the feature.

FEATURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the feature.

FEATURE_VERSION.description: Displays the version of the feature.

IS_DEPRECATED.description:
When set to TRUE, displays if a feature is deprecated, otherwise
it is FALSE.

OBJECT_COUNT.description:
Displays the counter tracking the number of artifacts that belong
to the feature. For example, thenumber of existing history
tables.

CALL_COUNT.description:
Displays the counter tracking the number of times the feature was
used since the last start of the indexserver. For example,
number of accesses to any history table.

LAST_TIMESTAMP.description:
Displays the last point in time when the feature was used. By
default, the value is deprecated if this configuration has not
changed.

LAST_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID of the last feature usage. By default,
the value is deprecated if this configuration has not
changed.

LAST_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the database user of the last feature usage, for example, SAP<SID>. By default, the value is deprecated
if this configuration has not changed.

LAST_APPLICATION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the application user of the last feature usage, for
example,<D-User>. By default, the value is
deprecated if this configuration has not changed.

LAST_APPLICATION_NAME.description:
Displays the application name of the last feature usage. By
default, the value is deprecated if this configuration has not
changed.

LAST_APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays the application source name and location of the last
feature usage. By default, the value is deprecated if this
configuration has not changed.

LAST_STATEMENT_ID.description:
Displays the statement ID of the last feature usage. By default,
the value is deprecated if this configuration has not
changed.

LAST_STATEMENT_HASH.description:
Displays the statement hash of the last feature usage. By
default, the value is deprecated if this configuration has not
changed.

M_FULLTEXT_COLUMN_STATISTICS.description: Tracks the usage of fulltext indexes.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name to which the table belongs.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the internal name of the text column.

LOOKUP_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of text lookups executed on this column.

LAST_LOOKUP_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the last lookup was executed.

M_FUZZY_SEARCH_INDEXES.description: Provides runtime information of fuzzy search indexes of column tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

INDEX_NAME.description: Displays the name of the index.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

FUZZY_SEARCH_MODE.description: Displays the fuzzy index search mode.

MIME_TYPE.description: Displays the MIME type of the fuzzy search index for search mode TEXT only.

TOKEN_SEPARATORS.description: Displays the token separators of the fuzzy search index for search mode TEXT only.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL.description:
Displays the sum of MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN and MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA in
bytes.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_MAIN.description:
Displays the current size of the fuzzy search indexes in main in bytes.
This value is 0 if not loaded.

MEMORY_SIZE_IN_DELTA.description:
Displays the current size of the fuzzy search indexes in delta, in bytes.
This value is 0 if not loaded.

LOADED.description:
Displays a flag to indicate that tge column is loaded into memory:
TRUE/FALSE.

M_GARBAGE_COLLECTION_STATISTICS.description: Provides garbage collection and history manager statistics.

STORE_TYPE.description: Displays the type of storage handled: COLUMN STORE/LIVECACHE.

HISTORY_COUNT.description:
Displays the current count of history files in the garbage
collection.

WAITER_COUNT.description: Displays the current count of garbage collection waiters.

MIN_READ_TID.description:
Displays the last known minimum transaction ID (TID) of a reading
transaction at end of transaction (EOT).

LAST_STARTED_TID.description: Displays the TID of the last started cleanup job.

LAST_STARTED_TID_POSTCOMMIT.description: Displays the TID of the last started postcommit job.

FIRST_WAITING_TID.description: Displays the TID of the first waiting cleanup job.

FIRST_WAITING_TID_POSTCOMMIT.description: Displays the TID of the first waiting postcommit job.

ENTERS.description:
Displays the number of history files that entered the queue for
cleanup.

ENTERS_POSTCOMMIT.description:
Displays the number of history files that entered the queue for
postcommit.

STARTED_JOBS.description: Displays the number of started garbage collection cleanup jobs.

STARTED_JOBS_POSTCOMMIT.description: Displays the number of started postcommit jobs.

PROCESSED_JOBS.description: Displays the number of undo files processed for cleanup.

PROCESSED_JOBS_POSTCOMMIT.description: Displays the number of undo files processed for postcommit.

QUEUE_LOADS.description: Displays the number of all garbage collection queue loads.

QUEUE_LOADS_NONEMPTY.description:
Displays the number of garbage collection queue loads which found
elements.

QUEUE_EMPTY_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of queue empty states after garbage collection
finished.

LAST_HISTORY_SIZE_AT_SVP.description:
Displays the lastest number of history files present at the
savepoint.

MAX_HISTORY_SIZE_AT_SVP.description:
Displays the maximum number of history files present at the
savepoint.

MIN_HISTORY_SIZE_AT_SVP.description:
Displays the minimum number of history files present at the
savepoint.

SUM_HISTORY_SIZE_AT_SVP.description: Displays the total number of history files present at the savepoint.

AVG_HISTORY_SIZE_AT_SVP.description:
Displays the average number of history files present at the
savepoint.

EMPTY_HISTORY_AT_SVP_COUNT.description: Displays the number of savepoints with an empty queue.

M_HA_DR_PROVIDERS.description: Provides information about loaded HA/DR providers.

PROVIDER_COMPANY.description: Displays the HA/DR provider company name.

EXECUTION_ORDER.description: Displays the HA/DR provider execution order.

STATISTICS_ID.description: Displays the statistics object unique ID.

INCLUSIVE_SIZE_IN_USE.description:
Displays the current size of this allocator, including suballocators in
bytes.

INCLUSIVE_COUNT_IN_USE.description:
Displays the number of blocks currently in use, including
suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description:
Displays the stotal allocated size in this allocator and suballocators in
bytes.

INCLUSIVE_DEALLOCATED_SIZE.description:
Displays the total deallocated size in this allocator and suballocators
in bytes.

INCLUSIVE_ALLOCATED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of allocations, including suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_DEALLOCATED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of deallocations, including suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_MAX_SINGLE_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description:
Displays the largest block size in bytes ever alllocated in this
allocator and suballocators.

INCLUSIVE_PEAK_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description:
Displays the largest size in bytes of this allocator and suballocators
(estimate).

INCLUSIVE_IN_USE_INTEGRAL.description:
Displays the average allocated memory in bytes by this allocator and its
suballocators, multiplied by the time since the start of measurement
(sample based rough estimate). Deactivated by default this value should
only be activated upon request by SAP support. UOM is 1 byte times 1
second.

EXCLUSIVE_SIZE_IN_USE.description: Displays the current size of this allocator in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_COUNT_IN_USE.description: Displays the number of blocks currently in use.

EXCLUSIVE_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the total allocated size in this allocator in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_DEALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the total deallocated size in this allocator in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_MAX_SINGLE_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description:
Displays the largest block size in bytes ever allocated in this
allocator.

EXCLUSIVE_PEAK_ALLOCATION_SIZE.description: Displays the largest size of this allocator (estimate) in bytes.

EXCLUSIVE_ALLOC_ERRORS.description: Displays the number of allocation errors.

EXCLUSIVE_IN_USE_INTEGRAL.description:
Displays the average allocated memory in bytes by this allocator,
multiplied by time since start of measurement (sample based rough
estimate). Deactivaetd by decault, this value should only be activated
upon request by SAP support. UOM is 1 byte times 1 second.

COMPONENT.description: Displays the name of SAP HANA component of this allocator.

AREA.description:
Identifies the part of the memory management being used, for
example, the PoolAllocator.

TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the total bytes in the area.

M_HISTORY_INDEX_LAST_COMMIT_ID.description: Provides the last commit ID of a history index for each session.

SESSION_OWNER_VOLUME_ID.description: Displays the volume ID of this session.

LAST_COMMIT_ID.description: Displays the last commit ID of history index in this session.

M_HOST_AGENT_INFORMATION.description: Displays computer system information retrieved from the SAP Host Agent.

CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the class.

INSTANCE_NUMBER.description:
Displays the number of instances per class, for example, if the host has multiple CPUs,
then all the properties of one CPU have the same instance
number.

PROPERTY_NAME.description: Displays the name of the property.

PROPERTY_VALUE.description: Displays the value of the property.

M_HOST_AGENT_METRICS.description: Displays performance metrics retrieved from the SAP Host Agent.

CLASS_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the class, which identifies the property as a CIM metric or a
SAPOSCOL metric.

INSTANCE_ID.description: Identifies the metric of a specific measured element.

DEFINITION_ID.description: Displays the ID of the metric.

TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the timestamp from the host agent when the metric instance was created.

CAPTION.description: Displays the name of the metric.

VALUE.description: Displays the value of the metric.

UNIT.description: Displays the unit of the metric (bytes, percent, and so on).

CIM_DATA_TYPE.description:
Displays a numerical value that identifies the data type of the metric as defined by the
CIM standard.

DESCRIPTION.description: Displays a detailed description of the metric.

MEASURED_ELEMENT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the measured element: CPU ID, file system name, network port name,
or disk name.

MEASURED_ELEMENT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of measured element (file system, operating system, and so on).

M_HOST_INFORMATION.description: Provides host information such as machine and OS configuration.

M_HOST_NETWORK_STATISTICS.description: Provides information about the network statistics of a host.

HOST.description: Displays the host name the network statistics refer to.

TCP_SEGMENTS_RECEIVED.description: Displays the total number of TCP segments received.

TCP_SEGMENTS_SENT_OUT.description: Displays the total number of TCP segments sent out.

TCP_SEGMENTS_RETRANSMITTED.description:
Displays the number of TCP segments that had to be
retransmitted.

TCP_BAD_SEGMENTS_RECEIVED.description:
Displays the number of TCP segments that were broken upon
receiving.

M_HOST_RESOURCE_UTILIZATION.description: Provides information about host resource utilization by all processes (including non-SAP HANA processes). CPU time is in milliseconds and added across all cores since system start.

FREE_PHYSICAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the free physical memory on the host in bytes.

USED_PHYSICAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the used physical memory on the host in bytes.

FREE_SWAP_SPACE.description: Displays the free swap memory on the host in bytes.

USED_SWAP_SPACE.description: Displays the used swap memory on the host in bytes.

ALLOCATION_LIMIT.description: Displays the allocation limit for all processes in bytes.

INSTANCE_TOTAL_MEMORY_USED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of memory from the memory pool that is currently
being used by SAP HANA processes in bytes.

INSTANCE_TOTAL_MEMORY_PEAK_USED_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool used by SAP HANA processes
since the instance started (this is a sample-based value) in bytes.

INSTANCE_TOTAL_MEMORY_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description:
Displays the size of the memory pool for all SAP HANA processes in
bytes.

INSTANCE_CODE_SIZE.description:
Displays the code size, including shared libraries of SAP HANA processes
in bytes.

INSTANCE_SHARED_MEMORY_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the shared memory size of SAP HANA processes in bytes.

TOTAL_CPU_USER_TIME.description: Displays the CPU time spent in the user mode in milliseconds.

TOTAL_CPU_SYSTEM_TIME.description: Displays the CPU time spent in the kernel mode in milliseconds.

TOTAL_CPU_WIO_TIME.description:
Displays the CPU time spent in wait I/O in milliseconds. Linux only,
Microsoft Windows is always 0.

TOTAL_CPU_IDLE_TIME.description: Displays the CPU idle time in milliseconds.

SYS_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the host timestamp in the local time zone.

UTC_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the host timestamp in UTC.

OPEN_FILE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of allocated file handles on the host. The Kernel
parameter isfs.file-nr. For more information, see
the OPEN_FILE_LIMIT key in the M_HOST_INFORMATION system view topic.

ACTIVE_ASYNC_IO_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of asynchronous input and/or output requests on the
host. The Kernel parameter isfs.ai-nr. For more
information, see the ASYNC_IO_LIMIT key in the M_HOST_INFORMATION system
view topic.

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of memory from the memory pool dedicated to the fast
restart option (FRO) that is mapped to SAP HANA processes.

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the size of the memory pool dedicated to the fast restart option
(FRO) that is owned by all SAP HANA processes.

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_PEAK_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to the fast
restart option (FRO) that was mapped to SAP HANA processes since the
start of the instance (sample-based value).

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_PEAK_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to the fast
restart option (FRO) that was owned by SAP HANA processes since the start
of the instance (sample-based value).

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of memory from the memory pool dedicated to
persistent memory that is currently mapped to SAP HANA processes.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of the memory pool dedicated to persistent memory
that is owned by all SAP HANA processes.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_PEAK_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to persistent
memory that was mapped to SAP HANA processes since the start of the
instance (sample-based value).

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_PEAK_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to persistent
memory that was owned by SAP HANA processes since the start of the instance
(sample-based value).

M_IMPORT_BINARY_STATUS.description: Provides import status information for the current session.

CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID to distinguish data of each import session (-1
for Python).

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the table or view being imported.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table or view being imported.

INDEX_TYPE.description:
Displays the index type: UNKNOWN, PHYSICAL, OLAP, JOIN, HIERARCHY, or
CALCULATION.

STATUS.description:
Displays the import status: QUEUED, WORKING, SKIPPED, DONE, or
FAILED.

ERROR.description:
Displays empty if the import was successful and displays error text
otherwise.

M_INDEXING_QUEUES.description: Provides the status of indexing queues.

QUEUE_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the queue: TEXT/GEOCODE.

INDEX_OID.description: Displays the OID of the corresponding index.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the data table.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the data table.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the document column.

STATUS.description: Displays the queue status: ACTIVE/SUSPENDED.

TOTAL_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of documents in the original table.

INDEXED_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of successfully indexed documents.

QUEUE_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of documents in the queue.

ERROR_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of failed documents.

M_INIFILES.description: Provides information about all configuration files.

DEFAULT_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the default layer.
This value is always TRUE.

SYSTEM_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the system layer:
TRUE/FALSE.

DATABASE_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the database
layer: TRUE/FALSE.

HOST_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the host layer:
TRUE/FALSE.

M_INIFILE_CONTENTS.description: Provides configuration information from INI files.

LAYER_NAME.description: Displays the configuration layer: DEFAULT, SYSTEM, HOST or DATABASE.

HOST.description:
Displays the host name, if the LAYER_NAME is HOST. Otherwise, this value
is NULL.

M_INIFILE_CONTENT_HISTORY.description: Provides change history information for configuration (ini) files.

TIME.description:
Displays the timestamp when the configuration setting was
changed.

FILE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the configuration file.

LAYER_NAME.description:
Displays the configuration layer: DEFAULT, SYSTEM, HOST, or
DATABASE.

HOST.description:
Displays the host name, if the LAYER_NAME is HOST. Otherwise,
this value is NULL.

USER_NAME.description:
Displays the user name if the change was made by an SAP HANA
user.

APPLICATION_NAME.description:
Displays the value of the APPLICATION session variable, if an
application made the change. Otherwise, this value is NULL.

APPLICATION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the value of the APPLICATIONUSER session variable, if an
application made the change. Otherwise, this value is NULL.

APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays the name of the application source if an application
made the change. Otherwise, this value is NULL.

SECTION.description:
Displays the name of the section in the configuration file where
the change was made.

KEY.description: Displays the name of the configuration key that was changed.

PREV_VALUE.description:
Displays the value of the configuration parameter before the
change was made.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the comment provided, if any, at the time of the configuration change.

MAX_CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.description:
Displays the maximum limit configured for concurrently running worker
threads.If this limit is exceeded, a dynamic reduction in the concurrently
running worker threads is automatically triggered, to prevent overload.

MAX_CONCURRENCY.description:
Displays the maximum number of concurrently running worker threads.The job executor keeps MAX_CONCURRENCY worker threads busy. As a result,
more worker threads are started if some worker threads are in a wait
state for some time. Even if these additional worker threads are
terminated, some of them are kept for later use and counted in
PARKED_WORKER_COUNT.

TOTAL_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of worker threads, including one extra worker
thread for emergency diagnostic purposes.

PARKED_WORKER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of parked worker threads.

FREE_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of worker threads that are idle and may
immediately take up a job to work on.

SYS_WAITING_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of worker threads waiting in system. A worker thread
may wait for any other kind of synchronization.

JOB_WAITING_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of worker threads waiting for a job on another worker
thread.

YIELD_WAITING_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of worker threads waiting on a yield. Sometimes a job
running on a worker thread yields execution to another job that is more
important.

TOTAL_WAITING_JOB_COUNT.description: Displays the number of all jobs waiting for execution.

QUEUED_WAITING_JOB_COUNT.description: Displays the number of jobs queued for execution.

WORKER_CREATE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of threads created.

M_JOB_HISTORY_INFO.description: Provides a history of long running system operations.

DISPLAY_LINE_COLOR.description: Displays the recommended display line color as RGB.

DISPLAY_LINE_STYLE.description: Displays the recommended display line style: 1=solid, 2=dotted, 3=dashed.

M_JOB_PROGRESS.description: Provides information about current long running system operations.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the object.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the object.

JOB_NAME.description: Displays the type of operation. See the table below for more detail.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection that triggered the operation.

START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the operation.

CURRENT_PROGRESS.description: Displays the steps of the operation that are already finished.

PROGRESS_DETAIL.description: Displays the detailed information about the operation.

M_JOINENGINE_STATISTICS.description: Provides statistics about join engine runtime objects used for column store join operations.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of translation tables.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory used by translation tables in bytes.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_UNLOAD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of translation table unloads.

M_JOIN_DATA_STATISTICS.description: Provides column store join engine join statistics.

SCHEMA_NAME1.description: Displays the schema name of the first table

TABLE_NAME1.description: Displays the name of the first table

COLUMN_NAME1.description: Displays the column name of the first table

SCHEMA_NAME2.description: Displays the schema name of the second table. Join data statistics are not collected

TABLE_NAME2.description: Displays the name of the second table.

COLUMN_NAME2.description: Displays the column name of the second table.

LAST_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the time when the statistics was last refreshed.

RECORD_COUNT1.description:
Displays the estimated total row count of the first table. For temporary tables, this
value is always 0.

COUNT1.description: Displays the number of not Null values of the column of the first table.

DISTINCT_COUNT1.description: Displays the number of unique not Null values of the column of the first table.

VALUE_COUNTS1.description:
Displays a comma-separated sorted list of unique column value counts. An incomplete
output of the list is truncated by an asterisk sign. If a
uniform distribution of column values is assumed, then there is
exactly one value count because values are uniformly distributed
and every value counts the same.

VALUE_COUNTS_FREQUENCIES1.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of frequencies of the first table's column values with
the same count. Any frequency refers to that value count with a
corresponding position in the list of value counts. An
incomplete output of the list is truncated by an asterisk sign.
If a uniform distribution of column values is assumed, then
there is exactly one value frequency because values are
uniformly distributed. Generally, this single frequency matches
the distinct count.

MATCHING_VALUES_COUNTS1.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of counts of the first table's column values which find a
join partner. Any matching values count refers to that value
count with corresponding position in the list of value counts.
An incomplete output of the list is truncated by an asterisk
sign. If a uniform distribution of column values is assumed,
then the matching values count the one and only value count that
is calculated from the unified distribution of the value
counts.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size, in bytes, used by the join data statistics object.

M_JOIN_TRANSLATION_TABLES.description: Provides column store join engine translation tables statistics.

COLUMN_NAME1.description: Displays the first table's column name.

PART_ID1.description:
Displays the first table partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

VERSION1.description: Displays the version information of the first table.

COLUMN_NAME2.description: Displays the second table's column name.

PART_ID2.description:
Displays the second table partition ID. Returns the
following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

VERSION2.description: Displays the version information of the second table.

IMPLEMENTATION_TYPE_CHANGE_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the implementation type was
changed.

IMPLEMENTATION_TYPE_CHANGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of implementation type changes.

VALUE_ID_COUNT1.description:
Displays the number of translated value IDs of the first
table.

VALUE_ID_COUNT2.description:
Displays the number of translated value IDs of the second
table.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory used by the translation table in bytes.

ESTIMATED_HASHMAP_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the estimated memory used by the HASHMAP implementation in bytes.

ESTIMATED_VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the estimated memory used by the vector implementation in bytes.

LAST_USED_COUNT.description:
Displays the counter to support LRU cache replacement
algorithm.

M_KERNEL_PROFILER.description: Displays the state and provides information about Kernel Profilers in the system. You must have the RESOURCE ADMIN or TRACE ADMIN system privileges to use this view.

APPLICATION_USER_NAME.description: Displays the application user name to filter on.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the SQL user name to filter on.

WAIT_TIME.description:
Deprecated. Displays the wait time between two sampling runs, in
milliseconds (SAMPLING_INTERVAL)

MEMORY_LIMIT.description:
Displays the limit for profiling data in bytes. This is not an exact
limit, a heuristic approach is used to limit memory.

SAMPLING_INTERVAL.description: Displays the sampling interval, in milliseconds.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID to filter on.

ROOT_STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the root statement hash to filter on.

TRACEPROFILE_NAME.description: Displays the user-specific trace profile name to filter on.

START_TIME.description: Displays the time that profiling was started.

STOP_TIME.description: Displays the time that profiling was stopped.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the current size, in bytes, of the kernel profile.

SAMPLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of samples stored for the profile.

STATUS.description:
Displays the status of the profiler. Possible values are: STARTED,
STOPPED

M_LANDSCAPE_HOST_CONFIGURATION.description: Specifies the host roles in a distributed landscape.

HOST_ACTIVE.description:
Displays the host active status which is a summary of the active values
of all the services on that host.

HOST_STATUS.description:
Displays the host status:OKThe host is operational; the host actual role equals the
configured role.IGNOREThe host is operational; the host configured as standby is
available, but not used.INFOThe host is operational; the host actual role is different from
the configured role.WARNINGThe host is not operational; the host should become operational
after startup/failover.ERRORThe host not operational; the host is in an erroneous state.

FAILOVER_STATUS.description:
Displays the failover status:(empty value)No failover is pending or active.waiting .. secA failure is detected. Waiting is required to prevent an
unnecessary failover. A restart of another host does not trigger
an immediate failover.waitingA failure is detected. Waiting for another starting host.failover to 'host'Failover to an active host named 'host'.failback to 'host'Failback to worker host active. This happens when a standby host
was assigned and is stopped. There is no automatic failback
while the standby host is assigned because this would cause
downtime.failedThe failover failed. Seenameserver.trcfor
details

FAILOVER_GROUP.description: Deprecated. This has been replaced by the FAILOVER_..._GROUP columns.

FAILOVER_CONFIG_GROUP.description:
Displays the configured failover group.Hosts can be grouped and during failover a host is looked for within the
same group. If no host is available in the same group, hosts from other
groups are used.

NAMESERVER_CONFIG_ROLE.description:
