IS_REPLICA.description: Displays the flag to indicate a replica.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the create time of the table.

CONTAINER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of containers for the table.

SCAN_COUNT.description: Displays the number of table scans.

M_RS_TABLE_VERSION_STATISTICS.description: Provides information on row table versions: detailed version counts and used sizes.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the schema to which the container belongs.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the table name of the container.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

CONTAINER_ID.description:
Indicates that some tables may have several containers to store its data.
The container ID is a unique identifier for each container.

IS_SYSTEM_TABLE.description: Indicates whether or not the table is a system table: TRUE/FALSE.

VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of all versions in the container. This includes all
versions regardless of their version type and status.

INSERT_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of insert-versions in the container.

UPDATE_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of update-versions in the container.

DELETE_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of delete-versions in the container.

COMMITTED_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of all committed versions in the container (includes
all types).

COMMITTED_INSERT_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of committed insert-versions in the container.

COMMITTED_UPDATE_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of committed update-versions in the container.

COMMITTED_DELETE_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of committed delete-versions in the container.

UNCOMMITTED_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of all uncommitted versions in the container
(includes all types).

UNCOMMITTED_INSERT_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of uncommitted insert-versions in the container.

UNCOMMITTED_UPDATE_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of uncommitted update-versions in the container.

UNCOMMITTED_DELETE_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of uncommitted delete-versions in the container.

VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of the versions in the container in bytes. This
includes all versions regardless of their version type and status.

INSERT_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of the insert-versions in the container in
bytes.

UPDATE_VERSION_SIZE.description: Displays the used size of update-versions in the container in bytes.

DELETE_VERSION_SIZE.description: Displays the used size of delete-versions in the container in bytes.

COMMITTED_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of all of the committed versions in the container
(includes all types) in bytes.

COMMITTED_INSERT_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of committed insert-versions in the container in
bytes.

COMMITTED_UPDATE_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of committed update-versions in the container in
bytes.

COMMITTED_DELETE_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of committed delete-versions in the container in
bytes.

UNCOMMITTED_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of all uncommitted versions in the container
(includes all types) in bytes.

UNCOMMITTED_INSERT_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of uncommitted insert-versions in the container in
bytes.

UNCOMMITTED_UPDATE_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of uncommitted update-versions in the container in
bytes.

UNCOMMITTED_DELETE_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the used size of uncommitted delete-versions in the container in
bytes.

MIN_COMMIT_ID.description:
Indicates that each committed version has its commit ID. MIN_COMMIT_ID
refers to the minimum value of commit ID among all committed
versions.

M_SAVEPOINTS.description: Displays current and historical savepoint statistics.

START_TIME.description: Displays the savepoint start time.

INITIATION.description: Displays the reason why the savepoint was executed.

PURPOSE.description: Displays the purpose of the savepoint.

STATE.description:
Displays the savepoint state. A savepoint is triggered periodically, but
it can be also triggered on demand using the ALTER SYSTEM SAVEPOINT
statement or by a data backup. The savepoint then runs through the
following various states:INITIAL- Displays the empty
statistics record (just allocated); immediately goes to thePREPAREstate.PREPARE- Displays that the savepoint
is preparing to run.PAGEFLUSH- flushes dirty pages
asynchronously.PAGESandSIZEcounters are updated to
reflect the savepoint progress.PRECRITICAL- runs any
pre-critical-phase callbacks and waits for I/O of the rest of the
flushed pages.ENTERCRITICAL- entering critical
phase: stops updaters and waits for the critical phase lock.CRITICAL- critical phase: copies and
triggers the write of any pages that are still dirty, records
various information in the restart record (log position,
transaction manager state, and so on), and increments the savepoint
version.PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_PHASEandSIZE_IN_CRITICAL_PHASEcounters are
updated appropriately.EXITCRITICAL- exit critical phase:
releases critical phase locks and restarts updaters.POSTCRITICAL- calls any post-critical
phase callbacks, finalizes the savepoint record, and cleans up
unneeded RTT entries.FINISHING- waits for the flush of
pages written in the critical phase and writes out the restart
record and anchor page.DONE- Displays that the savepoint has
been completed successfully.ABORTED- Displays that the savepoint
has been aborted (for example, due to a timeout during the
synchronization of the global savepoint).

REQUESTED_FREQUENCY.description: Displays the frequency of the savepoint.

TIME_SINCE_PREVIOUS.description:
Displays the time in seconds between the current and previous current
savepoints.

DURATION.description: Displays the total time in microseconds to create the savepoint.

PREPARE_FLUSH_RETRY_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of times the pages were flushed in the non-critical
phase before entering the critical phase.

BLOCKING_PHASE_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the last blocking phase.

BLOCKING_PHASE_DURATION.description: Displays the phase duration in microseconds.

CRITICAL_PHASE_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the last critical phase.

CRITICAL_PHASE_DURATION.description:
Displays the time in microseconds spent in the critical phase, during
which updates are blocked.

CRITICAL_PHASE_WAIT_TIME.description: Displays the wait time in microseconds for the critical phase.

TOTAL_SIZE.description:
Displays the number of bytes that have been prompted to be written to the
disk.

FLUSHED_PAGES.description: Displays the number of asynchronously flushed pages.

FLUSHED_PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description: Displays the number of pages flushed in the critical phase.

FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_PAGES.description: Displays the number of asynchronously flushed row store pages.

FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description: Displays the number of row store pages flushed in the critical phase.

FLUSHED_SIZE.description: Displays the size of asynchronously flushed pages in bytes.

FLUSHED_SIZE_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description: Displays the size in bytes of pages flushed in the critical phase.

FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_SIZE.description:
Displays the size in bytes of the asynchronously flushed row store
pages.

FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_SIZE_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description:
Displays the size of the row store pages flushed in the critical phase
.

RTT_SIZE.description:
Displays the total size in bytes of the rollback transaction table at the
savepoint.

SAVEPOINTS.description: Displays the number of executed savepoints.

START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the last savepoint.

INITIATION.description: Displays the reason why the last savepoint was executed.

PURPOSE.description: Displays the reason why the last savepoint was executed.

REQUESTED_FREQUENCY.description: Displays the currently active configured savepoint frequency in seconds.

LAST_FREQUENCY.description:
Displays the actual frequency in seconds between the last two
savepoints.

AVG_FREQUENCY.description:
Displays the actual average frequency in seconds between last two
savepoints.

LAST_DURATION.description:
Displays the total time spent in microseconds creating the last
savepoint.

AVG_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time spent in microseconds creating the last two
savepoints.

LAST_BLOCKING_PHASE_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the last blocking phase.

LAST_BLOCKING_PHASE_DURATION.description: Displays the duration in seconds of the last blocking phase.

LAST_CRITICAL_PHASE_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the last critical phase.

LAST_CRITICAL_PHASE_DURATION.description:
Displays the time spent in microseconds in the last critical phase,
during which updates are blocked.

AVG_CRITICAL_PHASE_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time spent in microseconds for the last two critical
phases, during which updates are blocked.

LAST_TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the total number of bytes written for the last savepoint.

AVG_TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the average number of bytes written for a savepoint.

LAST_FLUSHED_PAGES.description: Displays the last number of asynchronously flushed pages.

AVG_FLUSHED_PAGES.description: Displays the average number of asynchronously flushed pages.

LAST_FLUSHED_PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description: Displays the last number of pages flushed in the critical phase.

AVG_FLUSHED_PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description: Displays the average number of pages flushed in the critical phase.

LAST_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_PAGES.description: Displays the last number of asynchronously flushed row store pages.

AVG_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_PAGES.description:
Displays the average number of asynchronously flushed row store
pages.

LAST_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_.description:
Displays the last number of row store pages flushed in the critical
phase.

AVG_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_PAGES_IN_CRITICAL_P.description:
Displays the average number of row store pages flushed in the critical
phase.

LAST_FLUSHED_SIZE.description: Displays the size in bytes of the last asynchronously flushed pages.

AVG_FLUSHED_SIZE.description: Displays the average size in bytes of asynchronously flushed pages.

LAST_FLUSHED_SIZE_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description:
Displays the size in bytes of the last pages flushed in the critical
phase.

AVG_FLUSHED_SIZE_IN_CRITICAL_PHASE.description: Displays the average size of pages flushed in the critical phase.

LAST_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_SIZE.description:
Displays the size in bytes of the last asynchronously flushed row store
pages.

AVG_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_SIZE.description:
Displays the average size in bytes of the asynchronously flushed row
store pages.

LAST_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_SIZE_IN_CRITICAL_P.description:
Displays the size in bytes of the last row store pages flushed in the
critical phase.

AVG_FLUSHED_ROWSTORE_SIZE_IN_CRITICAL_PH.description:
Displays the average size in bytes of the row store pages flushed in the
critical phase.

LAST_RTT_SIZE.description:
Displays the total size in bytes of the rollback transaction table at
last savepoint. This may be less than the sum of components if there are
duplicate TIDs.

M_SCHEDULER_JOBS.description: Shows the status and the history of the status of the scheduled jobs.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the job.

USER_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the user who will execute, who is executing,
or who has executed the job.

SCHEDULER_JOB_NAME.description: Displays the name of the job.

STATUS.description:
Displays the status of the job. Valid statuses are:SCHEDULED if the job is scheduled for execution in the
futureRUNNING if the job is currently executingSUCCESS if the job executed successfullyERROR if an error occurred

ERROR_MESSAGE.description:
Displays an error message in the case where STATUS is
ERROR.

START_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the job is scheduled, if STATUS is
SCHEDULED; otherwise, the time when the job execution
started.

END_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the job execution ended if STATUS is
SUCCESS or ERROR; otherwise NULL.

KEY_TYPE.description: Displays the type of encryption key.

IS_CONSISTENT.description:
Indicates whether or not the key is consistent between the
persistence and the SSFS file: TRUE/FALSE.

RESET_COUNT.description: Displays the number of consistency resets.

VERSION.description: Displays the version of the key.

IS_CURRENT.description:
Indicates whether or not the key version is the current version
in the SSFS file: TRUE/FALSE.

STATISTICS_ID.description: Displays the statistics object unique ID.

WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of wait calls.

BLOCKING_COUNT.description: Displays the number of blocking wait calls.

LAST_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the last time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

MAX_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

MIN_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

SUM_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the total time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

AVG_BLOCKING_TIME.description: Displays the average time of blocking wait calls in microseconds.

CREATE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of semaphore creations (for shared statistics
only).

DESTROY_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of semaphore destructions (for shared statistics
only).

HOST.description:
Displays the host where the sequence cache exists. This is only displayed
when the cache is greater than 1 and the value has been cached.

PORT.description:
Displays the port where the sequence cache exists. This is only displayed
when the cache is greater than 1 and the value has been cached.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the sequence.

CACHE_SIZE.description: Displays the cache size of the sequence cache in bytes.

START_VALUE.description:
Displays the start value of the sequence cache. This is only displayed
when the cache is greater than 1 and the value has been cached.

END_VALUE.description:
Displays the end value of the sequence cache. This is only displayed when
the cache is greater than 1 and the value has been cached.

CURRENT_VALUE.description: Displays the current value of the sequence cache in bytes.

M_SERIES_TABLES.description: Provides statistics on the physical contents of series tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

SERIES_KEY_COUNT.description: Displays the number of distinct series identified by the SERIES KEY columns.

PERIOD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of distinct periods represented in the period column.

ROWS_EQUIDISTANT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of rows optimized to be in a piecewise equidistant segment.

ROWS_NOT_EQUIDISTANT_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that have not been optimized to be in a piecewise equidistant
segment.

ROWS_REORGANIZED_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that have been optimized by the ALTER TABLE SERIES REORGANIZE
statement.

ROWS_NOT_REORGANIZED_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of rows that have not been processed by the ALTER TABLE SERIES
REORGANIZE statement.

M_SERVICES.description: Provides the status of all services.

SERVICE_NAME.description:
Displays the service name. See M_SERVICE_TYPES for all known service
names.

DETAIL.description: Internal use only. Use COORDINATOR_TYPE to test the service role.

ACTIVE_STATUS.description: Displays the active status: NO, YES, UNKNOWN, STARTING, or STOPPING.

COORDINATOR_TYPE.description:
Displays the coordinator type in the distributed landscape: MASTER,
SLAVE, STANDBY, or NONE.

IS_DATABASE_LOCAL.description:
Displays whether or not the service is local to the database, tenant, or
if it belongs to one specific database.

M_SERVICE_COMPONENT_DETAILS.description: Allows closer monitoring of service components.

M_SERVICE_COMPONENT_HISTORY.description: Displays persisted information from the M_SERVICE_COMPONENTS and M_SERVICE_COMPONENT_DETAILS system views.

M_SERVICE_COMPONENT_MEMORY.description: Provides service-specific memory usage by logical component.

HOST.description: Displays the host where the service is running.

PORT.description: Displays the port where the service is running.

COMPONENT.description: Displays the logical component for which memory usage is reported.

USED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of memory, in bytes, being used for the logical
component.

M_SERVICE_COMPONENTS.description: Displays all known components and their current status within an SAP HANA service.

M_SERVICE_MEMORY.description: Displays detailed memory utilization information by services.

SENDER_HOST.description: Displays the host name of the sending service.

SENDER_PORT.description: Displays the port that the sending service listens on.

RECEIVER_HOST.description: Displays the host name of the receiving service.

RECEIVER_PORT.description: Displays the port that the receiving service listens on.

SEND_SIZE.description: Displays the number of bytes sent.

RECEIVE_SIZE.description: Displays the number of bytes received.

SEND_DURATION.description: Displays the time spent sending in microseconds.

RECEIVE_DURATION.description: Displays the time spent receiving in microseconds.

REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the number of requests handled.

M_SERVICE_NETWORK_METHOD_IO.description: Displays the number of calls and amount of data that is sent and received.

SENDER_HOST.description: Displays the host name of the sender.

SENDER_PORT.description: Displays the host port number of the sender.

RECEIVER_HOST.description: Displays the host name of the receiver.

RECEIVER_PORT.description: Displays the host port number of the receiver.

REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of requests for the method.

SEND_SIZE.description: Displays the total number of bytes sent.

RECEIVE_SIZE.description: Displays the total number of bytes received.

SECONDARY_SITE_ID.description: Displays the generated ID of the secondary site.

SECONDARY_SITE_NAME.description: Displays the secondary logical site name.

SECONDARY_CONNECT_TIME.description: Displays the time the connection was established from the secondary site.

SECONDARY_FULLY_RECOVERABLE.description:
Displays that the secondary system can be fully recovered with a backup
from the primary system.If this value is FALSE, then the backup history is broken. If there is a
takeover at that time, then start a new data backup once the takeover is
finished.

FULL_SYNC.description:
Displays the full sync status:DISABLED: the full sync is not configuredENABLED: the full sync is configured, but it is not yet active
(transactions do not block in this state)ACTIVE: the full sync mode is configured and active

LAST_LOG_POSITION_TIME.description: Displays the current log position timestamp.

LAST_SAVEPOINT_LOG_POSITION.description: Displays the current savepoint log position.

SHIPPED_LOG_POSITION_TIME.description: Displays the shipped log position timestamp.

SHIPPED_LOG_BUFFERS_COUNT.description: Displays the shipped log buffers count.

SHIPPED_LOG_BUFFERS_SIZE.description: Displays the shipped log buffers size in bytes.

SHIPPED_LOG_BUFFERS_DURATION.description: Displays the shipped log buffer duration in microseconds.

REPLAYED_LOG_POSITION.description:
Displays the log end position of the last known replayed log buffer on
the secondary site.

REPLAYED_LOG_POSITION_TIME.description:
Displays the timestamp of the last known replayed log buffer on the
secondary site.

SHIPPED_SAVEPOINT_LOG_POSITION.description: Displays the shipped savepoint log position.

SHIPPED_SAVEPOINT_START_TIME.description: Displays the shipped savepoint start time.

SHIPPED_FULL_REPLICA_COUNT.description: Displays the shipped full replica count.

SHIPPED_FULL_REPLICA_SIZE.description: Displays the shipped full replica size in bytes.

SHIPPED_FULL_REPLICA_DURATION.description: Displays the shipped full replica duration in microseconds.

SHIPPED_LAST_FULL_REPLICA_SIZE.description: Displays the shipped last full replica size in bytes.

SHIPPED_LAST_FULL_REPLICA_START_TIME.description: Displays the shipped last full replica start time.

SHIPPED_LAST_FULL_REPLICA_END_TIME.description: Displays the shipped last full replica end time.

SHIPPED_DELTA_REPLICA_COUNT.description: Displays the shipped delta replica count.

SHIPPED_DELTA_REPLICA_SIZE.description: Displays the shipped delta replica size in bytes.

SHIPPED_DELTA_REPLICA_DURATION.description: Displays the shipped delta replica duration in microseconds.

SHIPPED_LAST_DELTA_REPLICA_SIZE.description: Displays the shipped last delta replica size in bytes.

SHIPPED_LAST_DELTA_REPLICA_START_TIME.description: Displays the shipped last delta replica start time.

SHIPPED_LAST_DELTA_REPLICA_END_TIME.description: Displays the shipped last delta replica end time.

ASYNC_BUFFER_FULL_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of times the asynchronous replication buffer was
full.

BACKLOG_SIZE.description: Displays the current replication backlog in bytes.

MAX_BACKLOG_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum replication backlog in bytes.

BACKLOG_TIME.description: Displays the current replication backlog in microseconds.

MAX_BACKLOG_TIME.description: Displays the maximum replication backlog in microseconds.

REPLAY_BACKLOG_SIZE.description:
Displays the size, in bytes, of all log buffers that have been shipped to
the secondary site but have not yet been replayed on the scondary
site.

MAX_REPLAY_BACKLOG_SIZE.description:
Displays the maximumsize, in bytes, of the REPLAY_BACKLOG_SIZE since the
system startup.

REPLAY_BACKLOG_TIME.description:
Displays the time difference, in microseconds, between the time of the
last shipped log buffer and the last replayed log buffer on the secondary
site.

MAX_REPLAY_BACKLOG_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum time, in microseconds, of the REPLAY_BACKLOG_TIME
since the system startup.

SERVICE_NAME.description: Displays the service name.

DETAIL.description:
Displays the detail information, similar to COORDINATOR_TYPE in
M_SERVICES.

ACTIVE_STATUS.description:
Displays the active status. This column contains the following values:NO - Displays that the service has not started.YES - Displays that the service has started and is ready for
requests.UNKNOWN - Displays the initial state after starting a landscape, or
when the state is not known (for example, after a crash). If the
service does not start within a minute, this state changes to NO.STARTING - Displays that the service is starting. This state might
last for several minutes on first startup or recovery.STOPPING - Displays that the service is stopping.

START_TIME.description: Displays the process start timestamp.

PROCESS_CPU.description:
Displays the CPU usage percentage of the current process. See additional
notes after this table.

PROCESS_CPU_TIME.description:
Displays the CPU usage of the current process since the start in
milliseconds.

TOTAL_CPU.description:
Displays the CPU usage percentage of all processes. See additional notes
after this table.

TOTAL_CPU_TIME.description:
Displays the CPU usage of all processes since the start in milliseconds.
See additional notes after this table.

PROCESS_MEMORY.description: Displays the process logical memory usage in bytes.

PROCESS_PHYSICAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the process physical memory usage in bytes.

TOTAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the host physical and swap memory usage in bytes.

AVAILABLE_MEMORY.description: Displays the host physical and swap memory size in bytes.

PHYSICAL_MEMORY.description: Displays the host physical memory size in bytes.

REQUESTS_PER_SEC.description: Displays the requests per second. For internal use only.

RESPONSE_TIME.description: Displays the response time in milliseconds. For internal use only.

FINISHED_NON_INTERNAL_REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the number of finished requests. For internal use only.

ALL_FINISHED_REQUEST_COUNT.description:
Displays the finished requests, including internal requests. For internal
use only.

ACTIVE_REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the number of active requests. For internal use only.

PENDING_REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the number of pending requests. For internal use only.

ACTIVE_THREAD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of active threads.

THREAD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of total threads.

OPEN_FILE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of open files.

M_SERVICE_THREADS.description: Displays detailed information about threads created by services.

SERVICE_NAME.description:
Displays the service name. See M_SERVICE_TYPES for all known service
names.

HIERARCHY.description:
Displays the thread grouping information. This column contains the
connection ID, the update transaction ID, and the transaction ID. The
column is empty if the threads are inactive.

IS_ACTIVE.description:
Displays whether the thread is active (starting, running, stopping,
waiting, etc.).

DURATION.description: Displays the thread duration in milliseconds.

CALLER.description: Displays the service that called the thread.

CALLING.description: Displays the service called by the thread.

STATEMENT_ID.description: Displays the ID of the statement being executed.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for STATEMENT_STRING.

ROOT_STATEMENT_ID.description: Displays the ID of the root statement being executed.

ROOT_STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for the root statement string.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the SQL user name.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the application.

APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays that the application can define which source file SAP HANA is
called from. This value is also displayed in the
M_PREPARED_STATEMENTS.APPLICATION_SOURCE system view.

APPLICATION_COMPONENT_TYPE.description:
Can be populated by the application to provide context for the statement
execution: Displays which component type SAP HANA is called from.

APPLICATION_COMPONENT_NAME.description:
Can be populated by the application to provide context for the statement
execution: Displays which component name SAP HANA is called from.

CPU_TIME_SELF.description: Displays the active CPU time of the thread in microseconds.

CPU_TIME_CUMULATIVE.description:
Displays the active CPU time of the thread and its associated children in
microseconds.

CLIENT_IP.description: Displays the IP of the client machine.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction ID.

LOCK_WAIT_COMPONENT.description: Displays the component assigned to the lock.

LOCK_WAIT_NAME.description:
Displays the internal name of the lock (can be joined with the
STATISTICS_NAME column from, for example, M_MUTEXES).

LOCK_OWNER_THREAD_ID.description: Displays the ID of the thread that is holding the lock.

LOCKS_OWNED.description:
Displays the locks currently owned by the thread, including the lock type
(shared/exclusive). This column does not show all locks that are
monitored.

PASSPORT_ROOTCONTEXT_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) root context ID.

PASSPORT_TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) transaction ID.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) connection ID.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_COUNTER.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) connection counter.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_NAME.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component name.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component type.

PASSPORT_ACTION_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component action type.

PASSPORT_PREVIOUS_COMPONENT_NAME.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) passport component name of the
previous context.

PASSPORT_USER_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) user ID.

PASSPORT_TRACE_FLAGS.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) trace flags.

NUMA_NODE_INDEX.description: Displays the last known NUMA node that the thread was executed on.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the workload class.

M_SERVICE_THREAD_CALLSTACKS.description: Provides stack frame information for service threads.

SERVICE_NAME.description:
Displays the service name. See M_SERVICE_TYPES for all known service
names.

IS_ACTIVE.description: Displays whether the thread is active.

FRAME_LEVEL.description: Displays the level of the stack frame.

FRAME_NAME.description:
Displays the name of stack frame (function, file, library, and so
on).

M_SERVICE_THREAD_SAMPLES.description: Displays detailed information about locks held by threads.

SERVICE_NAME.description:
Displays the service name. See the M_SERVICE_TYPES system view for all
known service names.

HIERARCHY.description:
Displays the thread grouping information. This column contains the
connection ID, the update transaction ID, and the transaction ID. The
column is empty if the threads are inactive.

TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the timestamp of the record.

IS_ACTIVE.description: Displays whether the thread is active.

DURATION.description: Displays the duration of the operation in milliseconds.

CALLER.description: Displays the service, which called this thread.

CALLING.description: Displays the service, which the thread calls.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the unique identifier for an SQL string.

ROOT_STATEMENT_ID.description: Displays the ID of the root statement being executed.

ROOT_STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for the root statement string.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction ID.

UPDATE_TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the write transaction ID (this number increment).

USER_NAME.description: Displays the SQL user name.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the application.

APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays that the application can define which source file SAP HANA is
called from. The usage is up to the application. This value is also
displayed in M_PREPARED_STATEMENTS.APPLICATION_SOURCE.

APPLICATION_COMPONENT_TYPE.description:
Can be populated by the application to provide context for the statement
execution: Displays which component type SAP HANA is called from.

APPLICATION_COMPONENT_NAME.description:
Can be populated by the application to provide context for the statement
execution: Displays which component name SAP HANA is called from.

CPU_TIME_SELF.description: Displays the active CPU time of the thread in microseconds.

CPU_TIME_CUMULATIVE.description:
Displays the active CPU time of the thread and any associated children in
microseconds.

CLIENT_IP.description: Displays the IP of the client machine.

LOCK_WAIT_COMPONENT.description: Displays the waiting time for the lock component.

LOCK_WAIT_NAME.description:
Displays the waiting time for the lock ID (can be joined with
STATISTICS_NAME from, for example, M_MUTEXES).

LOCK_OWNER_THREAD_ID.description: Displays the waiting time for the thread ID.

LOCKS_OWNED.description:
Displays the locks currently owned by the thread, including the lock type
(shared/exclusive). Not all locks are monitored and show up here.

STATEMENT_THREAD_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective statement thread limit.

STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT.description: Displays the effective statement memory limit.

PASSPORT_ROOTCONTEXT_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) root context ID.

PASSPORT_TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) transaction ID.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) connection ID.

PASSPORT_CONNECTION_COUNTER.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) connection counter.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_NAME.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component name.

PASSPORT_COMPONENT_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component type.

PASSPORT_ACTION_TYPE.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) component action type.

PASSPORT_PREVIOUS_COMPONENT_NAME.description:
Displays the extended passport (EPP) passport component name of the
previous context.

PASSPORT_USER_ID.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) user ID.

PASSPORT_TRACE_FLAGS.description: Displays the extended passport (EPP) trace flags.

NUMA_NODE_INDEX.description: Displays the last known NUMA node that the thread was executed on.

WORKLOAD_CLASS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the workload class.

M_SERVICE_TRACES.description: Provides configured trace components for each service type.

SERVICE_NAME.description: Displays the service name.
Displays the service name.

INIFILE.description: Displays the configuration file name of service.

HAS_DETAIL.description:
Displays whether the service shows details in M_SERVICE_STATISTICS:
TRUE/FALSE.

M_SESSION_CONTEXT.description: Displays the session variables set for each connection.

KEY.description: Displays the key name of a session context variable.

VALUE.description: Displays the value of a session context variable.

SECTION.description: Displays the section name to distinguish system and user variables:USER=application defined variable using SET command or client API call.SYSTEM=predefined variable or server property.

M_SHARED_MEMORY.description: Provides shared memory usage information for the SAP HANA indexserver.

ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the allocated shared memory size on the module in bytes.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used shared memory size on the module in bytes.

FREE_SIZE.description: Displays the free shared memory size on the module in bytes.

PURPOSE.description: Displays why the snapshot was executed.

FOR_BACKUP.description: Displays if the snapshot was created for backup: TRUE/FALSE.

REDO_LOG_POSITION.description: Displays the redo log position corresponding to the snapshot.

LAST_COMMIT_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp of the last commit of the snapshot.

M_SQLSCRIPT_CODE_COVERAGE_OBJECT_DEFINIT.description: Provides definitions for the objects referenced in SQLScript code coverage results.

OBJECT_DEFINITION_ID.description: Displays the ID of the object definition.

OBJECT_DEFINITION.description: Displays the definition of the object.

M_SQLSCRIPT_CODE_COVERAGE_RESULTS.description: Provides per-session SQLScript code coverage results.

OBJECT_DEFINITION_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the object definition. This value corresponds to a value in
M_SQLSCRIPT_CODE_COVERAGE_OBJECT_DEFINITIONS.OBJECT_DEFINITION_ID.

DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the database.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the schema.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the object.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of object:FUNCTIONPROCEDUREANONYMOUS BLOCK

OBJECT_OID.description: Displays the ID of the object.

LINE.description: Displays the line number of the code snippet in the object definition.

COLUMN.description: Displays the starting column of the code snippet in the object definition.

START_POSITION.description: Displays the index of the code snippet's first character in the object definition.

END_POSITION.description: Displays the index of the code snippet's last character in the object definition.

HIT_COUNT.description:
Displays the hit count for the code snippet during a single invocation of the object;
zero if not covered.

INVOKER_OBJECT_DEFINITION_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the invoker's object definition; this value is NULL if this is the
top-most function or procedure.

INVOKER_LINE.description:
Displays the line number in the invoker's object definition; this value is NULL if this
is the top-most function or procedure.

INVOKER_COLUMN.description:
Displays the column in the invoker's object definition; this value is NULL if this is the
top-most function or procedure.

INVOKER_START_POSITION.description:
Displays the index of the first character in the invoker's object definition; this value
is NULL if this is the top-most function or procedure.

INVOKER_END_POSITION.description:
Displays the index of the last character in the invoker's object definition; this value
is NULL if this is the top-most function or procedure.

M_SQLSCRIPT_PLAN_PROFILERS.description: Lists the sessions and procedures that are currently being profiled by the SQLScript plan profiler.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

START_TIME.description:
Displays the timestamp that the ALTER SYSTEM ... SQLSCRIPT PLAN PROFILER statement was
executed.Displays the ID of the user who executed the ALTER
SYSTEM ... SQLSCRIPT PLAN PROFILER statement

PROCEDURE_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID if a FOR SESSION filter is specified, otherwise NULL.

PROCEDURE_SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the procedure schema name if a FOR PROCEDURE filter is specified, otherwise,
NULL.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the procedure name if a FOR PROCEDURE filter is specified.

HOST.description: Displays the host name where profiler is executed.

PORT.description: Displays the internal port where profiler is executed.

M_SQLSCRIPT_PLAN_PROFILER_RESULTS.description: Stores the results generated by the SQLScript plan profiler.

PROCEDURE_HOST.description: Displays the name of host where the outermost procedure started.

PROCEDURE_PORT.description: Displays the name of port where the outermost procedure started.

PROCEDURE_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID that started this outermost procedure.

PROCEDURE_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the database name of outermost procedure.

PROCEDURE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of schema of the outermost procedure.

PROCEDURE_LIBRARY_NAME.description: Displays the library name of the outermost procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the outermost procedure.

RESULT_ID.description: Displays the SQLScript profiler ID for the call.

OPERATOR_DETAILS.description:
Displays any key-value pairs of additional information that only belong to specific
operations.

START_TIME.description: Displays the start timestamp of the operation.

END_TIME.description: Displays the end timestamp of the operation.

DURATION.description: Displays the clock time, in microseconds, spent in the operation itself.

ACTIVE_TIME_SELF.description:
Displays the clock time, in microseconds, spent in the operation, excluding child
operations.

ACTIVE_TIME_CUMULATIVE.description: Displays the clock time, in microseconds, between START_TIME and END_TIME.

CPU_TIME_SELF.description:
Displays the CPU time, in microseconds, spent in the operation, excluding child
operations.

CPU_TIME_CUMULATIVE.description:
Displays the total CPU time, in microseconds, spent in the operation itself and its
children

USED_MEMORY_SIZE_SELF.description: Displays the memory used, in bytes, in the operation itself, excluding its children.

USED_MEMORY_SIZE_CUMULATIVE.description:
Displays the total memory used, in bytes, in the operation itself, including its
children.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID used for the operation.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the transaction ID used for the operation.

STATEMENT_ID.description: Displays the statement ID used for the operation.

THREAD_ID.description: Displays the thread ID used for the operation.

OPERATOR_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the database name of the procedure/function where the operator is defined.

OPERATOR_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the procedure/function where the operator is defined.

OPERATOR_LIBRARY_NAME.description: Displays the library name of the procedure/function where the operator is defined.

OPERATOR_PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the procedure/function where the operator is defined.

OPERATOR_LINE.description: Displays the SQL line of the operator.

OPERATOR_COLUMN.description: Displays the SQL column of the operator.

OPERATOR_POSITION.description: Displays the SQL position of the operator.

OPERATOR_HOST.description: Displays the host where the operation occurred.

OPERATOR_PORT.description: Displays the port where the operation occurred.

OPERATOR_ ID.description: Displays the ID of the operation.

PARENT_OPERATOR_ID.description: Displays the ID of the parent operation.

M_SQLSCRIPT_VARIABLE_CACHE.description: Provides runtime information about procedures and functions that have variable caches defined.

HOST.description: Displays the host address of the node on which the cached data is located.

PORT.description: Displays the port number of the node on which the cached data is located.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the target object.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the target object name.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the target object type: PROCEDURE/FUNCTION.

VARIABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the variable to be cached.

SQLSCRIPT_PLAN_ID.description: Displays the ID of the execution plan.

SQLSCRIPT_OPERATOR_ID.description: Displays the ID of the execution plan operator.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size of the cached data in bytes.

LAST_CACHE_TIME.description: Displays the date and time when the latest cached data was generated.

M_SQL_ANALYZER_PLAN_TRACES.description: Displays metadata of .plv files generated by the PlanTracer. This information is provided for PUBLIC users.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user who executed the statement.

SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the schema that the SQL plan belongs to. SQL
plans are generated in each schema even though the statement
string is the same since the query optimizer statistics might be
different.

STATEMENT_EXECUTION_ID.description: Displays the unique statement execution ID.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the statement hash of the query string.

EXECUTION_START_TIME.description: Displays the statement execution start time.

EXECUTION_END_TIME.description: Displays the statement execution end time.

M_SQL_CLIENT_NETWORK_IO.description: Provides the client and server elapsed time as well as message sizes for client network messages.

CLIENT_HOST.description:
Displays the host of the client machine, also found in
M_CONNECTIONS.CLIENT_HOST.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID of the physical server connection.

MESSAGE_ID.description: Displays the client message ID on the physical server connection.

CLIENT_DURATION.description:
Displays the elapsed time on the client side including send, server
execution, and receive.

SERVER_DURATION.description:
Displays the execution time on the server side. Transport time is the
delta between CLIENT_DURATION and SERVER_DURATION.

SERVER_RECEIVED_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the message was received on the server side and
begin of execution.

RECEIVED_MESSAGE_SIZE.description: Displays the size of messages received on the server side in bytes.

SEND_MESSAGE_SIZE.description: Displays the size of the messages sent to the client side in bytes.

M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE.description: Provides statistics for an individual execution plan.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for the STATEMENT_STRING column.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name who prepared the plan.

SESSION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the session user name who prepared the plan. This is the same
value appears for USER_NAME in the M_CONNECTIONS.USER monitoring
view.

SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the schema name that the SQL plan belongs to. SQL plans are
generated in each schema even though the statement string is the same,
since the query optimizer statistics might be different.

SESSION_PROPERTIES.description: Displays the composed variables from the session context.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether a plan is currently validated (TRUE/FALSE). A plan is
invalidated when its corresponding schema objects, such as table and
view, are changed. Invalidated plans are recompiled if the same statement
is executed; otherwise, they are evicted by another plan when newly
compiled.

LAST_INVALIDATION_REASON.description: Displays the reason for the last invalidation.

IS_INTERNAL.description:
Displays TRUE if the query plan was initiated from a database internal
connection, not from the user nor other servers. For example, when an
internal SAP HANA component executes a SQL statement related to the
Statistics Service (_SYS_STATISTICS), or runs SQL statements in a
background thread that exploit SQL Plan Cache, and so on.Displays FALSE if the query plan was initiated by a client application.

IS_DISTRIBUTED_EXECUTION.description:
Displays whether tables are located in multiple nodes or multiple
services are involved in plan execution (TRUE/FALSE).

COMPILATION_OPTIONS.description:
Displays the compilation options that are used by the SQL Query Optimizer
when it generates cached execution plans:Returns an empty string if the SQL Query Optimizer compiled without
any special settingsReturns a STATEMENT HINT option when the SQL Query Optimizer compiles
and optimizes with a statement hintReturns an ABSTRACT SQL PLAN option when the SQL Query Optimizer
compiles with an abstract sql planReturns a STATEMENT HINT ON INNER STATEMENT option when the SQLScript
compiler applies a statement hint to an inner statement of this
SQLScript plan.Multiple options can used together to create a list of options, for
example: STATEMENT HINT, ABSTRACT SQL PLAN.

IS_PINNED_PLAN.description:
Displays whether the plan is explicitly stored by the PlanViz utility
(TRUE/FALSE).

ABAP_VARCHAR_MODE.description:
Displays whether the ABAP VARCHAR mode is enabled (TRUE/FALSE). Only ABAP
application developers are concerned with this mode, which indicates if
it is a NULL terminated string or not.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the application name information.

ACCESSED_TABLES.description:
Displays the relevant base table ID list accessed in the plan. The format
is ( ) for a list entry, with entries separated by commas.

ACCESSED_TABLE_NAMES.description:
Displays the relevant base table name list accessed in the plan. The
format is ( ) for a list entry, with entries separated by commas.

ACCESSED_OBJECTS.description:
Displays the relevant schema object ID list accessed in the plan. The
format is ( ) for a list entry, with entries separated by commas.

ACCESSED_OBJECT_NAMES.description:
Displays the relevant schema object name list accessed in the plan. The
format is ( ) for a list entry, with entries separated by commas.

TABLE_LOCATIONS.description:
Displays all relevant table locations for both original and replicated
tables.The table location contains the host, port, database ID, and volume ID,
for examplelddbqm0:30240, 0, 3.

TABLE_TYPES.description:
Displays whether the plan refers to the column store only, row store
only, or mixed. Possible values are: ROW, COLUMN, or ROW, COLUMN.

EXECUTION_ENGINE.description: Displays the list of all involved execution frameworks.

HEX_REJECTION_REASON.description: Displays the reason for not using the HEX engine.

PLAN_SHARING_TYPE.description: Displays the plan sharing type: GLOBAL, SESSION LOCAL, or LOGICAL SESSION GLOBAL.See below for definitions.

OWNER_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection owning the plan.

PLAN_ID.description: Displays the logical plan ID, which is a non-negative value.

PLAN_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size used by the plan in bytes.

REFERENCE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of statements using the plan. When the number reaches
zero, it can be evicted by the victim selection policy of the plan
cache.

PARAMETER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of parameters assigned to the execution.

UPDATED_TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the updated table for the plan.

LOGICAL_CONNECTION_VOLUME_ID.description:
Displays the volume ID of the logical connection. This value is 0 if
there is no session context defining it as a global plan.

EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated count of the plan execution.

EXECUTION_COUNT_BY_ROUTING.description:
Displays the accumulated count of the plan execution by a client-routed
connection in the statement routing. This column shows how many times the
statement is executed in the routed connection. The routed connection is
a physical connection in a part of the logical session.

TOTAL_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the sum of time of the plan execution in microseconds, including
the communication time with clients.

AVG_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time of the plan execution in microseconds,
including the communication time with clients.

MIN_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the minimum time of the plan execution in microseconds,
including the communication time with clients.

MAX_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum time of the plan execution in microseconds,
including the communication time with clients.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time of the plan executions in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the plan execution in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of the plan execution in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the plan execution in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time in microseconds establishing result sets.

AVG_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the average time that the cursor is open in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time for cursor open in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time for cursor open in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time for transferring rows in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the average time for cursor fetch in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time for cursor fetch in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time for cursor fetch in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time for result set cleanup in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the average time for cursor close in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time for cursor close in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time for cursor close in microseconds.

TOTAL_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the sum of the table loading time during plan preparation in
microseconds.

AVG_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the average table loading time during plan preparation in
microseconds.

MIN_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the minimum table loading time during plan preparation in
microseconds.

MAX_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the maximum table loading time during plan preparation in
microseconds.

PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of plan preparations.

TOTAL_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

AVG_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

MIN_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

MAX_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

TOTAL_RESULT_RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated number of records during plan execution.

TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated lock wait count for the plan.

TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_DURATION.description:
Displays the accumulated lock wait duration for the plan in
microseconds.

LAST_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the last connection ID that executed the plan.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the sum of memory peaks of all plan executions, in bytes.In cases where a query was executed in a scale-out system (that is, the
query was running distributed across multiple hosts), then
TOTAL_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE is the highest peak memory usage across all
involved hosts). So, if host A had a peak usage of 2 GB and host B has a
peak usage of 3 GB, then MEMORY_SIZE displays 3 GB.

AVG_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the average memory peak of all plan executions, in bytes.

MIN_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the minimum memory peak of all plan executions, in bytes.

MAX_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum memory peak of all plan executions, in bytes.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the total amount of tracked CPU time.

AVG_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the average amount of tracked CPU time.

MIN_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the minimum amount of tracked CPU time.

MAX_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the maximum amount of tracked CPU time.

AVG_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_COUNT.description:
Displays the average count of service network requests during plan
execution.

MAX_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_COUNT.description:
Displays the maximum count of service network requests during plan
execution.

TOTAL_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_COUNT.description:
Displays the total count of service network requests during plan
execution.

AVG_CALLED_THREAD_COUNT.description:
Displays the average count of called threads used during plan
execution.

MAX_CALLED_THREAD_COUNT.description:
Displays the maximum count of called threads used during plan
execution.

TOTAL_CALLED_THREAD_COUNT.description:
Displays the total count of called threads used during plan
execution.

TOTAL_BATCH_EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays a total batch count per one execution for cached DML plans.

AVG_BATCH_EXECUTION_COUNT.description:
Displays an average batch count per one execution for cached DML
plans.

MIN_BATCH_EXECUTION_COUNT.description:
Displays a minimum batch count per one execution for cached DML
plans.

MAX_BATCH_EXECUTION_COUNT.description:
Displays a maximum batch count per one execution for cached DML
plans.

AVG_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time in microseconds of service network requests
during plan execution.

MAX_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum time in microseconds of service network requests
during plan execution.

TOTAL_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_DURATION.description:
Displays the total time in microseconds of service network requests
during plan execution.

AVG_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_SIZE.description:
Displays the average data amount in bytes of service network requests
during plan execution.

MAX_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_SIZE.description:
Displays the maximum amount in bytes of service network requests during
plan execution.

TOTAL_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_SIZE.description:
Displays the total amount in bytes of service network requests during
plan execution.

TOTAL_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_HIT_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of times the NSE page was found in buffer cache
memory.

AVG_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_HIT_COUNT.description:
Displays the average number of times page the NSE page was found in
buffer cache memory.

MIN_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_HIT_COUNT.description:
Displays the minimum number of times the NSE page was found in buffer
cache memory.

MAX_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_HIT_COUNT.description:
Displays the maximum number of times the NSE page was found in buffer
cache memory.

TOTAL_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_MISS_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of times the NSE page had to be read from
disk.

AVG_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_MISS_COUNT.description:
Displays the average number of times the NSE page had to be read from
disk.

MIN_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_MISS_COUNT.description:
Displays the minimum number of times the NSE page had to be read from
disk.

MAX_BUFFER_CACHE_PAGE_MISS_COUNT.description:
Displays the maximum number of times the NSE page had to be read from
disk.

TOTAL_BUFFER_CACHE_PINNED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the total size of pinned the NSE page memory out of buffer
cache.

AVG_BUFFER_CACHE_PINNED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the average size of pinned the NSE page memory out of buffer
cache.

MIN_BUFFER_CACHE_PINNED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the minimum size of pinned the NSE page memory out of buffer
cache.

MAX_BUFFER_CACHE_PINNED_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
Displays the maximum size of pinned the NSE page memory out of buffer
cache.

TOTAL_BUFFER_CACHE_IO_READ_SIZE.description: Displays the total size of NSE pages read from disk into the buffer cache.

AVG_BUFFER_CACHE_IO_READ_SIZE.description: Displays the average size of NSE pages read from disk into the buffer cache.

MIN_BUFFER_CACHE_IO_READ_SIZE.description: Displays the minimum size of NSE pages read from disk into the buffer cache.

MAX_BUFFER_CACHE_IO_READ_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum size of NSE pages read from disk into the buffer cache.

M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE_AUTO_RECOMPILATION_STAT.description: Provides information about triggered auto recompilations.

PLAN_ID.description: Displays the logical plan ID, which is a non-negative value.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for STATEMENT_STRING.

PREPARATION_COUNT_BY_AUTO_RECOMPILATION.description: Displays the number of plan preparations by automatic recompilation based on execution statistics.

PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of plan preparations.

M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE_EXECUTION_ENGINE_STATIS.description: Displays execution statistics information. For some plan IDs, two entries for a single query string are returned, one each for HEX and non-HEX.

PLAN_ID.description: Displays the logical plan ID which is a non-negative value.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for STATEMENT_STRING.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name who prepared the plan.

SESSION_USER_NAME.description: Displays the SESSION_USER name who prepared the plan. The same value with M_CONNECTIONS.USER_NAME

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name that the SQL plan belongs to. SQL plans are generated in each schema even though the statement string is the same since the query optimizer statistics might be different.

EXECUTION_ENGINE.description: Displays a list of all involved execution frameworks.

PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated count of plan preparation.

EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated count of plan execution.

AVG_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of plan preparation in milliseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average elapsed time of plan execution in milliseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the average CPU time of plan execution in milliseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the average size of tracked actual memory consumption in bytes.

M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE_EXECUTION_LOCATION_STAT.description: Provides statistics for hosts where plans were executed.

PLAN_ID.description: Displays the logical plan ID as a non-negative value.

EXECUTION_HOST.description: Displays the host where the plan was executed.

EXECUTION_PORT.description: Displays the port where the plan was executed.

TOTAL_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the total size of the tracked memory consumption in bytes.

AVG_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the average size of the tracked memory consumption in bytes.

MIN_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the minimum size of the tracked memory consumption in bytes.

MAX_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum size of the tracked memory consumption in bytes.

M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE_OVERVIEW.description: Provides overall statistics of evicted and cached plans

PLAN_CACHE_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the SQL Plan Cash is enabled (TRUE/FALSE).ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('indexserver.ini','system') SET('sql',
'plan_cache_enabled') = <'True' or 'False'> WITH RECONFIGURE.

STATISTICS_COLLECTION_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the runtime statistics are being collected
(TRUE/FALSE).ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('indexserver.ini','system') SET('sql',
'plan_cache_statistics_enabled') = <'True' or 'False'> WITH
RECONFIGURE.

PLAN_CACHE_CAPACITY.description:
Displays the maximum SQL Plan Cache size in bytes.ALTER SYSTEM ALTER CONFIGURATION ('indexserver.ini','system') SET('sql',
'plan_cache_size') = '268435456' WITH RECONFIGURE.

CACHED_PLAN_SIZE.description: Displays the total size of SQL Plan Cache in bytes.

PLAN_CACHE_HIT_RATIO.description: Displays the SQL Plan Cache hit ratio.

PLAN_CACHE_LOOKUP_COUNT.description: Displays the number of plan lookup counts from SQL Plan Cache.

PLAN_CACHE_HIT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of hit counts from SQL Plan Cache.

EVICTED_PLAN_AVG_CACHE_TIME.description:
Displays the average duration in microseconds between plan cache
insertion and eviction.

EVICTED_PLAN_COUNT.description: Displays the number of evicted plans from SQL Plan Cache.

EVICTED_PLAN_PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the total plan preparation count for evicted plans.

EVICTED_PLAN_EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the total plan execution count for evicted plans.

EVICTED_PLAN_PREPARATION_TIME.description:
Displays the total duration in microseconds for plan preparation for all
evicted plans. It is the sum of the evicted plan's
TOTAL_PREPARATION_TIME.

EVICTED_PLAN_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the total cursor duration in microseconds for evicted plans. It
is the sum of the evicted plan's TOTAL_CURSOR_DURATION.

EVICTED_PLAN_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description:
Displays the total execution time in microseconds for evicted plans. It
is the sum of th evicted plan's TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.

EVICTED_PLAN_SIZE.description: Displays the accumulated total size of evicted plans in bytes.

CACHED_PLAN_COUNT.description: Displays the total cached plan count in SQL Plan Cache.

CACHED_PLAN_PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the total plan preparation count for cached plans.

CACHED_PLAN_EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the total execution count for cached plans.

CACHED_PLAN_PREPARATION_TIME.description:
Displays the total plan preparation duration for cached plans in
microseconds.

CACHED_PLAN_CURSOR_DURATION.description: Displays the total cursor duration for cached plans in microseconds.

CACHED_PLAN_TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the total execution time for cached plans in microseconds.

CLEAR_TIMESTAMP.description:
Displays the timestamp when SQL Plan Cache was cleared for the last time
using ALTER SYSTEM CLEAR SQL PLAN CACHE.

M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE_PARAMETERS.description: Provides bind parameters for statements cached in SQL Plan Cache. It saves a parameter set of the most expensive execution for each plan.

PLAN_ID.description:
Displays the logical plan ID which is a non-negative value, same with the
one in M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE.

DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the data type of the parameter.

POSITION.description: Displays the position of the parameter in SQL statement.

PARAMETER_VALUE.description: Displays the value of the captured parameter.

BATCH_EXECUTION_ORDER.description: Displays the execution order in a batch processing.

EXECUTION_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the timestamp when the parameter value is captured.

M_SQL_PLAN_VARIANT_STATISTICS.description: Provides statistics for Plan Variant, a method that enables the caching of multiple plans (variant plans) for each parameterized query.

LAST_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the last connection ID that executed the plan.

PLAN_ID.description: Displays the logical plan id used in the SQL plan cache.

PLAN_VARIANT_ID.description: Displays the logical plan variant id used in plan variant cache.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for the STATEMENT_STRING column.

VARIANT_FILTERS.description: Displays the list of variant filters.

PREPARATION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of plan preparations

LAST_PREPARATION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the tracked actual memory. consumption of last preparation

TOTAL_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

AVG_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

MIN_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

MAX_PREPARATION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the plan preparation in microseconds.

EXECUTION_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated count of the plan execution.

TOTAL_CURSOR_DURATION.description: Displays the sum of time of the plan execution, including the communication time with clients, in microseconds

AVG_CURSOR_DURATION.description: Displays the average time of the plan execution, including the communication time with clients, in microseconds.

MIN_CURSOR_DURATION.description: Displays the minimum time of the plan execution, including the communication time with clients, in microseconds.

MAX_CURSOR_DURATION.description: Displays the maximum time of the plan execution, including the communication time with clients, in microseconds

TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time of the plan executions in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the plan execution in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of the plan execution in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the plan execution in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time establishing result sets in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the average time that the cursor is open in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time for cursor open in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time for cursor open in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time for transferring rows in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the average time for cursor fetch in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time for cursor fetch in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time for cursor fetch in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the sum of time for result set cleanup in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the average time for cursor close in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time for cursor close in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time for cursor close in microseconds.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the total size of tracked actual memory consumption.

AVG_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the average size of tracked actual memory consumption in bytes.

MIN_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the minimum size of tracked actual memory consumption in bytes.

MAX_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum size of tracked actual memory consumption in bytes.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the total amount of tracked CPU time in microseconds.

AVG_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the average amount of tracked CPU time in microseconds.

MIN_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the minimum amount of tracked CPU time in microseconds.

MAX_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the maximum amount of tracked CPU time in microseconds.

TOTAL_RESULT_RECORD_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated number of records during plan execution.

TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description: Displays the accumulated lock wait count for the plan.

TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_DURATION.description: Displays the accumulated lock wait duration in microseconds for the plan.

AVG_CALLED_THREAD_COUNT.description: Displays the average count of called threads used during plan execution.

MAX_CALLED_THREAD_COUNT.description: Displays the maximum count of called threads used during plan execution.

TOTAL_CALLED_THREAD_COUNT.description: Displays the total batch count per one execution for cached DML plans.

AVG_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the average count of service network requests during plan execution.

MAX_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the maximum count of service network requests during plan execution.

TOTAL_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_COUNT.description: Displays the total count of called threads used during plan execution.

AVG_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time of the service network requests during plan execution in
microseconds.

MAX_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum time of the service network requests during plan execution in
microseconds.

TOTAL_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum time of the service network requests during plan execution in
microseconds.

AVG_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_SIZE.description: Displays the average data amount of service network requests during plan execution in bytes.

MAX_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_SIZE.description: Displays the maximum amount of service network requests during plan execution in bytes.

TOTAL_SERVICE_NETWORK_REQUEST_SIZE.description: Displays the total amount of service network requests during plan execution in bytes.

M_SQL_PLAN_STATISTICS.description: Provides statistics of a live or evicted individual execution plan.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for STATEMENT_STRING.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name who prepared the plan.

SESSION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the SESSION_USER name who prepared the plan. The same
value as M_CONNECTIONS.USER_NAME.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name that the SQL plan belongs to.

SESSION_PROPERTIES.description:
Displays the composed variables that can be changed by session
context.

IS_VALID.description: Displays whether the plan is currently valid: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_EVICTED.description: Displays whether the plan has already been evicted: TRUE/FALSE.

LAST_INVALIDATION_REASON.description: Displays the last plan invalidation reason.

LAST_EVICTION_REASON.description: Displays the last plan eviction reason.

IS_INTERNAL.description:
Displays whether the plan is executed from a database internal
connection (TRUE) or from a remote connection (FALSE).

IS_DISTRIBUTED_EXECUTION.description: Displays whether the tables are located in multiple nodes: TRUE/FALSE.

COMPILATION_OPTIONS.description:
Displays the compilation options that are used by the SQL Query
Optimizer when it generates cached execution plans:Returns an empty string if the SQL Query Optimizer compiled
without any special settingsReturns a STATEMENT HINT option when the SQL Query Optimizer
compiles and optimizes with a statement hintReturns an ABSTRACT SQL PLAN option when the SQL Query
Optimizer compiles with an abstract sql planMultiple options can be used together to create a list of
options, for example: STATEMENT HINT, ABSTRACT SQL PLAN.

IS_PINNED_PLAN.description: Displays whether the plan is explicitly stored by PlanViz utility: TRUE/FALSE.

PINNED_PLAN_ID.description:
If this plan is pinned, dispalys the same value as
PINNED_SQL_PLANS.ID.

ABAP_VARCHAR_MODE.description: Displays whether ABAP VARCHAR mode is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the application name information.

ACCESSED_TABLES.description:
Displays the relevant base table ID list accessed in the
plan.

ACCESSED_TABLE_NAMES.description:
Displays the relevant base table name list accessed in the
plan.

ACCESSED_OBJECTS.description:
Displays the relevant table object ID list accessed in the
plan.

ACCESSED_OBJECT_NAMES.description:
Displays the relevant schema object name list accessed in the
plan.

TABLE_LOCATIONS.description: Displays the relevant table locations for the plan.

TABLE_TYPES.description:
Displays whether the plan refers Column store only, Row store
only or mixed.

OWNER_CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection owning the plan. The possible values
are:GLOBAL: -1 (always)SESSION EXCLUSIVE GLOBAL: -1, owner connection ID, which
is a positive valueSESSION LOCAL: owner connection ID, which is a positive
value (never changed)

PLAN_ID.description:
Displays the logical plan ID, which is a non-negative value. If
the plan is evicted, -1 is shown.

PLAN_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size used by the plan in bytes.

EVICTION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of evictions that have occurred for the
plan.

LAST_EVICTION_TIMESTAMP.description:
Displays the timestamp when the plan was evicted for the last
time. Returns zero if the plan has never been evicted.

REFERENCE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of statements using the plan.

PARAMETER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of parameters to be assigned for the
execution.

UPDATED_TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table changed by the plan.

LOGICAL_CONNECTION_VOLUME_ID.description:
Displays the volume ID of the logical connection. This value is 0
if there is no session context.

EXECUTION_COUNT.description:
Displays the accumulated count of plan execution for valid and
evicted plans.

EXECUTION_COUNT_BY_ROUTING.description:
Displays the accumulated count of plan execution by client-routed
connection in statement routing for valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the sum of time, in microseconds, that cursors were opened. This includes plan
execution and communication time with clients for valid and
evicted plans.

AVG_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the average time, in microseconds, of the plan execution, including
communication time with clients for valid and evicted plans.

MIN_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the minimum time, in microseconds, of the plan execution, including
communication time with clients for valid and evicted plans.

MAX_CURSOR_DURATION.description:
Displays the maximum time, in microseconds, of the plan execution, including
communication time with clients for valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME.description:
Displays the sum of time, in microseconds, of the plan execution for valid and evicted
plans.

AVG_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the average time of the plan execution.

MIN_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the minimum time of the plan execution.

MAX_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Displays the maximum time of the plan execution.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description:
Displays the sum of time in microseconds for cursor open for
valid and evicted plans.

AVG_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description:
Displays the average time in microseconds for cursor open for
valid and evicted plans.

MIN_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description:
Displays the minimum time in microseconds for cursor open for
valid and evicted plans.

MAX_EXECUTION_OPEN_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum time in microseconds for cursor open for
valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description:
Displays the sum of time in microseconds for cursor fetch for
valid and evicted plans.

AVG_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description:
Displays the average time in microseconds for cursor fetch for
valid and evicted plans.

MIN_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description:
Displays the minimum time in microseconds for cursor fetch for
valid and evicted plans

MAX_EXECUTION_FETCH_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum time in microseconds for cursor fetch for
valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description:
Displays the sum of time in microseconds for cursor close for
valid and evicted plans.

AVG_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description:
Displays the average time in microseconds for cursor close for
valid and evicted plans.

MIN_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description:
Displays the minimum time in microseconds for cursor close for
valid and evicted plans.

MAX_EXECUTION_CLOSE_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum time in microseconds for cursor close for
valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the sum of table loading time during plan preparation
for valid and evicted plans.

AVG_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the average table loading time in microseconds during
plan preparation for valid and evicted plans.

MIN_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the minimum table loading time during plan preparation
for valid and evicted plans.

MAX_TABLE_LOAD_TIME_DURING_PREPARATION.description:
Displays the maximum table loading time in microseconds during
plan preparation for valid and evicted plans.

PREPARATION_COUNT.description:
Displays the count of plan preparations for valid and evicted
plans.

TOTAL_PREPARATION_TIME.description:
Displays the sum of time in microseconds of plan preparation for
valid and evicted plans.

AVG_PREPARATION_TIME.description:
Displays the average time in microseconds of plan preparation for
valid and evicted plans.

MIN_PREPARATION_TIME.description:
Displays the minimum time in microseconds of plan preparation for
valid and evicted plans.

MAX_PREPARATION_TIME.description:
Displays the maximum time in microseconds of plan preparation for
valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_RESULT_RECORD_COUNT.description:
Displays the accumulated number of records during plan execution
for valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_COUNT.description:
Displays the accumulated lock wait count for the plan for valid
and evicted plans.

TOTAL_LOCK_WAIT_DURATION.description:
Displays the accumulated lock wait duration in microseconds for
the plan for valid and evicted plans.

LAST_CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the last connection ID that executed the plan.

LAST_EXECUTION_TIMESTAMP.description:
Displays the timestamp when the plan was executed for the last
time.

LAST_PREPARATION_TIMESTAMP.description:
Displays the timestamp when the plan was compiled for the last
time.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
With memory tracking, displays the average size in bytes of
tracked actual memory consumption for valid and evicted
plans.

AVG_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
With memory tracking, displays the maximum size in bytes of
tracked actual memory consumption for valid and evicted
plans.

MIN_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
With memory tracking, displays the minimum size in bytes of
tracked actual memory consumption for valid and evicted
plans.

MAX_EXECUTION_MEMORY_SIZE.description:
With memory tracking, displays the total size in bytes of tracked
actual memory consumption for valid and evicted plans.

TOTAL_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the total amount of tracked CPU time.

AVG_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the average amount of tracked CPU time.

MIN_EXECUTION_CPU_TIME.description: Displays the minimum amount of tracked CPU time.
